Created MotherDuck Dive via MD_CREATE_DIVE (id: e5328c74-1d35-4d5c-ad1e-3ad4a0b25007) — Monday Command Center with L1 streak, calendar, overdue tasks, actionable emails. | Decisions: Subagent C DuckDB write path is the root cause of empty calendar/email tables — needs structural fix in am-backend-parallel.md or the subagent prompt itself. Dive queries ps_analytics live so it self-heals as data populates.

[2026-04-06] Topic: Fix AM-Backend Subagent C DuckDB writes + fix Dive rendering | Actions: Created email-calendar-duckdb-sync.md protocol (explicit SQL templates, column mappings, sender classification, execution order with DuckDB-first). Updated am-backend-parallel.md Subagent C spec to reference new protocol + added l1_streak refresh and data_freshness updates to Phase 5. Updated am-auto.kiro.hook prompt with Subagent C DuckDB emphasis. Fixed Dive v2: corrected useSQLQuery data format (array of objects not rows array), column-name access, N() BigInt helper, dollar-quoted SQL, fully qualified table names. | Decisions: Root cause of empty email/calendar tables was missing sync protocol file — Subagent B1 had 300-line asana-duckdb-sync.md, Subagent C had two bullet points. l1_streak now refreshed during AM-Backend Phase 5 (not just EOD-2). DuckDB writes are primary deliverable for all ingestion subagents, file output is secondary fallback.

[2026-04-06] Topic: Karpathy Autoresearch Lab Dive | Actions: Explored karpathy schema (autoresearch_experiments 115 rows/25 organs/7 techniques, autoresearch_priors 189 rows, organ_health 7 organs, experiment_outcomes, run_summary view). Created MotherDuck Dive (id: d46b341a-21c4-4d82-b379-a95f0647d789) with 6 panels: summary KPIs, organ stacked bar chart, technique effectiveness chart, Bayesian prior convergence bars, organ health word count chart, recent experiments feed. Used correct useSQLQuery patterns (column-name access, N() helper, dollar-quoting, fully qualified table names). | Decisions: Dive queries ps_analytics.karpathy live — no static data. REWORD highest volume (35) at 100% keep; REMOVE highest revert rate (63%). Brain/memory/device most experimented organs.

[2026-04-06] Topic: Karpathy Dive v2 — master KPI trend charts | Actions: Queried time-series data (3 days: Apr 2-4, 115 experiments). Updated Dive to v2 with 3 master ComposedChart panels at top: (1) cumulative experiments/keeps/reverts area+line, (2) daily keep rate % + cumulative keep % + organs touched dual-axis, (3) cumulative word delta (net compression trending -4080w) + daily volume bars. | Decisions: Used ComposedChart with Area+Line+Bar combos and dual Y-axes. Data will enrich as more EOD runs accumulate daily data points.

[2026-04-06] Topic: Karpathy Dive v3 — master KPI charts + prior evolution | Actions: Replaced previous charts with 4 new master panels: (1) Total Words vs Cumulative Experiments dual-axis line, (2) Latency/Spinning over time (avg + P90 seconds + agent calls) with worst-spinner grid (au-context ADD, memory RESTRUCTURE, brain ADD at 120s), (3) Output Quality (kept vs reverted scores + delta A-B + keep rate bars), (4) Bayesian Prior Evolution — 4 mini line charts (REWORD/COMPRESS/ADD/REMOVE) showing posterior convergence per organ over experiment count, reconstructed from running window functions on experiments table. | Decisions: Prior history reconstructed from experiment decisions (no historical snapshot table exists) using cumulative alpha/beta window functions. Filtered to top 7 organs × 4 techniques for readability.

[2026-04-06] Topic: Karpathy Dive v4 — autoresearch-style experiment log table | Actions: Fetched karpathy/autoresearch GitHub Discussion #43 to study the exact table format (val_bpb | Delta | Status | Description). Rebuilt Dive v4: replaced old organ/technique bar charts with compact 3-column master charts (Words vs Experiments, Latency/Spinning with worst spinners, Output Quality), 4-column Bayesian prior evolution grid, and full experiment log table (115 rows) modeled after Karpathy's format — Score A, Delta, KEEP/REVERT badges, organ, technique, word delta, wall clock, section+notes. | Decisions: Experiment log is the primary content (mirrors autoresearch results.tsv format). Charts compressed to top row. Delta computed as score_a - score_b (positive = improvement). Green row highlighting for KEEP decisions.

[2026-04-06] Topic: Karpathy Dive v5-v6 — running best chart + composite efficiency metric | Actions: Added Karpathy-style "Score Over Experiments" hero chart (v5) with green/red dots per experiment and step-after running best line. Then replaced raw score_a with composite Efficiency Score (v6): quality × (1 − 0.3×latency/120s) × (1 − 0.2×tokens/15K) × (1 + 0.2×compression/613w). Range 0.44–0.91 across 115 experiments. Running best shown as filled area. Rich tooltip with full breakdown. Efficiency column added to experiment log table. | Decisions: Efficiency formula weights: 30% latency penalty, 20% token cost penalty, 20% compression bonus. Ideal = 1.0. Normalization bounds from actual data ranges (120s max, 15K tokens max, 613w max delta). spine REWORD at 0.91 is current efficiency leader.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Team Testing Dashboard Dive — structured experiment catalog | Actions: Read 7 test documents (Yahoo JP NB, Google JP NB, JP Apple App, IT Bid Modifiers, Audience Post-mortem, DG Test Results, PS Measurement) + oci-performance.md + ad-copy-results.md + current.md + change_log. Created ps.team_experiments table (22 columns) and populated with 24 experiments spanning 2022-2026 across 10 regions. Created MotherDuck Dive (id: df173821-0ba2-47bc-86d7-8018517e92d5) with region aggregation bar chart, Richard vs Team comparison panel, per-owner breakdown, and full experiment log table with region/status/verdict badges. Richard's rows highlighted blue with ★ marker. | Decisions: Experiments attributed by owner from source docs. is_richard flag separates Richard's 17 tests from 7 team-only tests. Verdicts assigned: 12 WIN, 3 LOSE, 4 MIXED, 4 PENDING, 1 INCONCLUSIVE. Table is append-only — new experiments get added as they complete.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Testing Dashboard v3 — restructured attribution + scale classification | Actions: Added test_scale column (program/experiment/pilot) and sole_owner to team_experiments. Created experiment_contributors junction table (36 rows) with owner/contributor roles — excluded stakeholders (Alex, Mao, Data Science, Vijay), kept only PS team (Richard, Stacey, Brandon, Andrew, Yun, Adi). Rebuilt Dive v3: region chart now shows scale not win/loss, split to two attribution panels (Sole Owners with win rate bars + Contributor Impact with role breakdown), two separate tables (Programs vs Experiments & Pilots), people column with ★ owner markers. Fixed DATE→strftime bug from v1. | Decisions: Programs = multi-market long-running (OCI, Polaris, Email Overlay). Experiments = A/B with control. Pilots = exploratory/small. Richard is sole owner on 10 tests, contributor on 2 more. Stacey sole owner on 3 (OCI markets). Region chart deliberately avoids win/loss to prevent misleading market-ownership attribution.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Testing Dashboard v4 — market-level attribution | Actions: Created experiment_markets junction table (58 rows) expanding EU3→FR/IT/ES, EU5→UK/DE/FR/IT/ES, WW→all 10 markets. Updated Dive v4: region chart now shows all 10 individual markets with accurate per-market test counts, tables show colored market badges per row, contributor impact shows markets_touched from junction table. | Decisions: WW tests (bid strategy, Polaris, email overlay) count in all 10 markets. No more aggregate region labels (EU3/EU5/NA/APAC) — only specific markets or WW.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker — DuckDB schema + prediction dashboard | Actions: Created 3 DuckDB tables (channel_actuals, channel_predictions, prediction_scores), seeded 13 weeks MX PS + 13 weeks AU PS actuals, 30 predictions with confidence intervals, scored 15 predictions; built standalone HTML dashboard at ~/shared/dashboards/forecast-tracker.html with actual-vs-predicted charts, confidence interval bands, accuracy bars, Brier-style calibration bubble chart, and full scorecard table. Paid App Marketing included as placeholder (app installs/cost/CPI). | Decisions: Brier component calculated as normalized squared error (not binary Brier); calibration chart plots stated confidence vs CI hit rate against perfect-calibration diagonal; weekly data entry workflow feeds predictions at weekly/monthly/quarterly/year_end horizons.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Testing Dashboard v10 — attribution integrity overhaul | Actions: Audited all 36 contributor assignments against source docs. Removed guessed Richard contributions (OCI US/UK/DE, SyRT US/UK). Marked 13 uncertain assignments as 'unconfirmed' (Email Overlay, Gated Guest, OCI non-Stacey roles, Polaris WW, Promo Test, Ad Copy Andrew). Updated Dive: cross-tab and timeline now only count confirmed roles, new yellow "Unconfirmed Roles" section lists every test×person needing Richard's input, people column shows ★ confirmed / ? unconfirmed. | Decisions: High-confidence attributions kept (Richard's own test docs = 10 tests). Medium/low confidence demoted to unconfirmed rather than guessed. Richard to fill gaps. Principle: if unsure, label N/A — don't inflate attribution from Asana assignments or Slack mentions.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Testing Dashboard — attribution corrections from Richard | Actions: Applied Richard's confirmed attributions: Ad Copy→Andrew owner (not Richard), Email Overlay + Polaris WW→Richard sole owner, OCI UK/DE→Richard contributor (planning) + Andrew contributor + Stacey owner, OCI US→Stacey owner + Andrew contributor (Richard not involved), Promo→Andrew sole, Gated Guest→Brandon owner + Richard contributor. Cleared all 13 unconfirmed roles to 0. Updated is_richard flags. | Decisions: 28 confirmed contributor rows, 0 unconfirmed. Richard owns 12 tests (down from 14 after ad copy correction). Baloo noted as future Richard-primary test (not yet in table).

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Testing Dashboard — attribution corrections from Richard | Actions: Applied 8 corrections: Ad Copy UK/IT → Andrew owner (was Richard), Email Overlay → Richard sole owner confirmed, Polaris WW → Richard sole owner (Stacey removed), OCI UK/DE → Richard contributor (planning) + Andrew contributor, OCI US → Andrew contributor only, Promo Test → Andrew sole owner, Gated Guest → Brandon owner + Richard contributor. Cleared all 13 unconfirmed roles to 0 remaining. Updated is_richard flags on team_experiments. | Decisions: Attribution line: owner = planned and executed the test end-to-end. Contributor = meaningfully involved in planning or execution. Neither = just aware or gave feedback. Richard owns 12 tests, contributes to 3 more (OCI UK/DE planning, Gated Guest).

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Testing Dashboard — interactive filters + final attribution cleanup | Actions: Added interactive filter bar with Person/Market/Year pill slicers using React useState + useMemo client-side filtering. All tables and KPI counts update on click. Consolidated all data into single allData query for filter efficiency. Also applied EU5 aggregation to contributor×market pivot, owner-first ordering in people column (ORDER BY role in STRING_AGG), and multiple attribution corrections across sessions: bid strategy WW→AU, Polaris US→Stacey, OCI UK/DE→Richard owner, OCI EU3→Richard owner, Adi added to OCI JP/CA. | Decisions: Client-side filtering chosen over SQL re-query (24 rows too small to justify server round-trips). Filters stack (person+market+year). Clear button resets all. Dive now at v13 with 24 experiments, 3 junction tables (contributors, markets, experiments), fully confirmed attributions.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker — MotherDuck Dive setup | Actions: Created 3 base tables (channel_actuals, channel_predictions, prediction_scores) in ps_analytics on MotherDuck, seeded 26 actuals (13 MX, 13 AU) + 30 predictions + 15 scored predictions; built 6 Dive-ready views (v_forecast_combined, v_forecast_future, v_forecast_accuracy, v_forecast_calibration, v_forecast_kpis, v_forecast_timeseries); also built standalone HTML dashboard at ~/shared/dashboards/forecast-tracker.html before pivot to MotherDuck. | Decisions: Views designed to map 1:1 to MotherDuck Dive widgets (line chart, area bands, bar chart, scatter calibration, KPI cards, scorecard table). Brier component = normalized squared error. Paid App Marketing placeholder ready for app installs/cost/CPI. Confidence calibration plots stated confidence vs CI hit rate against perfect-calibration diagonal.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Testing Dashboard v15 — global filter propagation | Actions: Refactored all charts and tables from separate SQL queries to client-side useMemo derivations from single filtered dataset. Timeline chart, contributor×region pivot, market bar chart, and owners panel all now recompute from filtered rows. Removed 3 independent SQL queries (timelineQ, pivot, byMarket, soleOwners) replaced with 4 useMemo computations. | Decisions: Client-side derivation chosen over parameterized SQL re-queries — 24 rows is trivially fast to recompute in JS. All panels now globally filter-aware. Multi-select filters (Set-based) with OR within dimension, AND across dimensions.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker — MotherDuck Dive (built for real) | Actions: Discovered existing `ps` schema with empty metrics/forecasts/projections tables + markets/channels reference data; populated ps.metrics (78 rows: MX+AU weekly registrations/cost/cpa) and ps.forecasts (30 rows: scored+unscored weekly/monthly/quarterly predictions); created 4 Dive-specific views (dive_forecast_timeline, dive_forecast_scorecard, dive_forecast_calibration, dive_forecast_kpis); built and deployed React Dive via MD_CREATE_DIVE with interactive channel/metric toggles, SVG chart with CI bands, accuracy bars, calibration table, and full scorecard; cleaned up redundant main schema tables/views from prior session. | Decisions: Used existing ps schema infrastructure instead of duplicating in main; Dive ID f39a3470-d7cf-45f4-b1d0-2bde437e948c is 4th dive alongside Command Center, Karpathy Lab, and Testing Dashboard. Paid App placeholder uses market='PA', channel='pa'.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker Dive — multi-horizon predictions + layout redesign | Actions: Inserted 45 new forecasts (MX+AU monthly Apr-Jun, quarterly Q1-Q4, year-end) into ps.forecasts; scored existing March monthly and Q1 quarterly predictions against actuals (MX March regs MISS 740 vs 930, MX Q1 regs HIT 2100 vs 2194, AU March regs MISS 420 vs 531); updated Dive to v2 — removed redundant accuracy bars, added separate Monthly/Quarterly/Year-End sections each with horizontal bar chart (CI bands + predicted line + actual dots) and scorecard table. | Decisions: Each period_type gets its own visualization section rather than a single combined scorecard. Scored predictions now include real misses which improves calibration honesty. Year-end shown as big-number display with tracking status.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker Dive v4 — drift tracking, narratives, multi-horizon layout | Actions: Created ps.forecast_revisions table + ps.dive_forecast_drift view for projection drift tracking; created ps.forecast_narratives table with 10 narrative justifications (weekly/monthly/quarterly/year-end/revisions for MX+AU); seeded 75 R1 revision rows from existing forecasts; inserted 24 AU predictions (monthly Apr-Jun, quarterly Q1-Q4, year-end) + scored AU Q1/March and MX March/Q1 predictions; updated Dive to v4 with drift tables in monthly/quarterly/year-end sections, narrative justifications per section, and revision notes at bottom. Hit repeated empty-param MCP errors on large payloads — resolved by minifying the JSX component. | Decisions: Drift table shows revision history per target period with ↑/↓ drift direction, % change, and vs-actual error at each revision point. Narratives stored in DB (not hardcoded) so they update independently of the Dive code. Revision trigger rule: 2+ consecutive weeks deviating >10% from prediction cascades to monthly/quarterly/year-end revisions.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker Dive — React error #31 fix (DATE type rendering) | Actions: Recreated 3 views (dive_forecast_drift, dive_forecast_scorecard, dive_forecast_timeline) with all DATE columns cast to VARCHAR; verified zero DATE/TIMESTAMP/INTERVAL types remain in any dive_* view. | Decisions: Root cause was DuckDB returning DATE values as `{days: N}` objects which React can't render as children. Fix is view-level (CAST AS VARCHAR), no Dive code change needed. Learned: always cast DATE columns to VARCHAR in views consumed by MotherDuck Dives.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker Dive v5 — charts replacing tables, drift visualization | Actions: Rebuilt Dive with SVG LineChart component reused across monthly/quarterly/year-end sections; each section now has two charts: (1) forecast chart with predicted values + CI bands + actual dots per period, (2) drift chart showing prediction evolution over revision dates. Removed table-only approach. | Decisions: Drift chart x-axis = revision date (when prediction was made), not target period — shows convergence over time. Year-end section keeps big-number display plus drift chart below. Tables removed in favor of charts; scorecard data still queryable via views but not rendered in Dive.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker Dive v6 — combined forecast+drift charts | Actions: Created ps.dive_forecast_combined view merging scorecard and drift data; rebuilt Dive with single ForecastChart component per section combining current predictions (orange triangles), CI bands, actuals (blue dots), error lines, and ghost circles for prior revisions at same x-position with drift path lines connecting them. Removed separate drift charts. | Decisions: One chart per section instead of two — forecast and drift coexist on same SVG. Ghost circles (gray hollow) show prior revision values vertically at each period's x-position; drift path connects them when 2+ revisions exist. Error visualization uses dashed vertical lines between actual and predicted, color-coded by magnitude.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker Dive v7 — cumulative model rebuild | Actions: Created ps.cumulative_actuals view (running YTD/QTD/MTD sums + rolling avg for CPA); created ps.cumulative_projections table with 26 cumulative endpoint projections (monthly/quarterly/year-end for MX+AU regs/cost/cpa); rebuilt Dive around single unified timeline chart — blue actual line growing cumulatively, dashed projection lines fanning from last actual to monthly (purple), quarterly (orange), year-end (red) endpoints with CI bars; added toggle buttons to show/hide each horizon layer; tables below per horizon show revision history with reason. | Decisions: Fundamental model shift from standalone period predictions to cumulative endpoints on shared timeline. CPA uses rolling average instead of cumulative sum. Old ps.forecasts/forecast_revisions tables retained for historical scoring but Dive now reads from cumulative_projections. Revision workflow: insert new row with revision_number+1 when projection changes.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker Dive v8 — React error fix + data pipeline gap identified | Actions: Fixed "returnReact is not defined" error by rewriting all arrow functions as function declarations and ensuring proper JSX spacing to prevent minification collisions; confirmed ps.dashboard_uploads table exists but is empty — no automated WW dashboard → DuckDB pipeline exists yet; added footer note flagging manual seed status. | Decisions: Actuals currently manual seed only; WW dashboard pipeline scoped as separate future task pending format clarification (Excel/CSV/API). Arrow functions in Dive JSX are unreliable under MotherDuck's minifier — use function declarations instead.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker Dive v9 — full year chart + CI bands + actuals pipeline plan | Actions: Added 18 cumulative projections for Jul-Dec (MX regs/cost, AU regs) so all 12 months have endpoints; rebuilt chart with full Jan-Dec x-axis, month labels, vertical grid lines; added layered CI bands as shaded SVG paths fanning from last actual — purple/monthly most opaque, orange/quarterly medium, red/year-end lightest; projection lines connect monthly endpoints through year-end. | Decisions: CI band colors use opacity layering (0.12/0.09/0.06) so bands stack visually without clutter. Actuals pipeline: Richard will drop weekly WW dashboard file to intake/ for parsing into ps.metrics. Footer updated to reflect this workflow.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker Dive v10 — single-select view switcher | Actions: Replaced multi-toggle (Monthly/Quarterly/Year-End) with single-select radio-style buttons (Weekly/Monthly/Quarterly/Year-End); chart now shows only the selected horizon's projection line + CI band alongside the always-visible actual cumulative line; KPI cards, projection table, and narrative all adapt to selected view. | Decisions: Single-select reduces visual clutter — one projection layer at a time. Each view has its own color identity (blue/purple/orange/red). Weekly view shows actuals only with no projections. Projection table only renders for non-weekly views.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker Dive v11 — weekly predictions, nested tables, column rename | Actions: Updated Dive with KPI cards always visible (all 4 endpoints shown regardless of view); renamed "Cum Target" to "Predicted"; weekly view now shows weekly scorecard with Predicted/Actual/CI/Error/Grade; quarterly table nests months indented under each quarter; year-end table nests quarters then months with double-indent (0px/16px/32px padding hierarchy). | Decisions: All four KPI cards persist across views so weekly view has prediction context. Table structure adapts per view — weekly shows flat scorecard, monthly shows flat months, quarterly/year-end show hierarchical nesting with parent rows bold+tinted and child rows gray.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker Dive v12 — persistent full-year table across all views | Actions: Replaced view-dependent tables with single "Full Year Projection Breakdown" table that always shows Year-End → Q1-Q4 → Jan-Dec hierarchy with 3-level indentation (0/16/32px) and color-coded depth (red year-end, orange quarters, gray months); view toggle now only controls chart projection lines/CI bands. | Decisions: Table is view-independent — always shows complete hierarchy. Chart view toggle is purely visual (which projection layer to highlight). Separation of concerns: chart = focused view, table = full context.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker Dive v13 — all layers always visible, yellow CI, emphasis toggle | Actions: Rebuilt chart to show all three projection layers (monthly/quarterly/year-end) simultaneously with view toggle controlling emphasis (full opacity + thick lines vs 35% opacity + thin lines); CI bands changed to yellow for all layers; full Year-End→Q1-Q4→Jan-Dec table always visible regardless of view; weekly view now shows actuals with all projection layers visible in background. | Decisions: View toggle is emphasis control, not visibility control — everything always renders. Yellow CI bands with opacity layering (0.12 monthly, 0.09 quarterly, 0.06 year-end). Labels only render for the emphasized layer to reduce clutter.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker Dive v14 — three-layer x-axis (weeks/months/quarters) | Actions: Rebuilt chart x-axis with 3 rows: week numbers (W1-W52 every 4th), month names with span lines, quarter labels with bracket lines and end caps; expanded bottom padding to 85px; added all 52 week dates to allDates array for full-year even spacing; month boundaries shown as dashed vertical grid lines. | Decisions: Week labels show every 4th week to avoid clutter. Month row uses horizontal span line with centered label. Quarter row uses bracket notation (horizontal bar + vertical end caps) with bold label. Three-layer axis provides context at every zoom level.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker Dive v15 — hover tooltips, layout cleanup, actuals in table | Actions: Added SVG hover tooltips (value/CI/type) with crosshair; moved metric filter below market filter; simplified chart to weekly actuals + monthly projections only (removed quarterly/year-end lines); removed view toggle; added time period to KPI box titles ("Month - Apr", "Quarter - Q2", "Year - 2026"); inverted x-axis darkness (weeks darker, quarters lighter); added Actual + Error columns to table with Q1 actuals (Jan/Feb/Mar/Q1) for MX+AU; inserted 8 Q1 cumulative projections for comparison. | Decisions: Chart shows only monthly horizon — quarterly/year-end live in KPI boxes only. Actuals hardcoded for MX Q1 (596/1264/2194) pending dynamic pipeline. Error column color-coded green/yellow/red by magnitude. View toggle removed as unnecessary with single-chart approach.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker Dive v16 — legend fix, hover tooltips, March error fix, weekly table, N/A handling | Actions: Fixed Q1 monthly projections to reflect actual predictions (MX Feb 1200 vs 1264 actual, Mar 2100 vs 2194 — March now shows ~4.3% error instead of 0%); simplified legend to Actual/Predicted/CI; hover tooltips show predicted+actual+CI for hovered point; added W1-W52 weekly projections table with scrollable container and sticky header; inserted 7 weekly cumulative projections (W09-W11/W14 MX, W09-W10/W14 AU); unpredicted periods now show "N/A" instead of using actuals. | Decisions: Weekly table uses 50% opacity for rows with no data. Tooltip flips left/right based on chart position. N/A is the standard display for any period without a prediction — error column shows dash when no prediction exists to compare against.

[2026-04-06] Topic: Polaris LP switch — when told Alexis | Actions: Slack search, email search to find the message | Decisions: Found DM to Alexis on 3/24 confirming same-day Polaris LP switch for AU

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker Dive v17 — justification reorder, calibration by horizon, Dive edit pattern discovered | Actions: Updated dive_forecast_calibration view to group by horizon (weekly/monthly/quarterly/year_end scored separately); moved Justification section above Full Year Projection table using SQL REPLACE on existing content via temp table; added all narrative sections (revisions/monthly/quarterly/year_end) to justification block; calibration table now shows horizon in parentheses. Discovered critical pattern: `SET VARIABLE` + `getvariable()` bypasses MotherDuck's "table function cannot contain subqueries" limitation — enables SQL surgery on existing Dive content without re-emitting full 19KB component. | Decisions: Brier scores now separate per horizon (option 1 — simplest). Weighted composite deferred until more data. SET VARIABLE pattern is the go-to for future large Dive edits — avoids the empty-param MCP failures that plague direct inline pushes.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker Dive v18 — CI on KPI boxes, grouped calibration table | Actions: Added CI ranges under Quarter and Year-End KPI boxes via SQL REPLACE surgery; restructured calibration table to group by horizon (weekly/monthly/quarterly section headers with metric rows underneath) instead of per-row parentheses; used SET VARIABLE + getvariable() pattern for push. | Decisions: Calibration grouped by horizon with bold capitalized section headers and colSpan rows as visual separators. Quarter box now matches Month box format (value + CI range).

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker Dive v19 — inline tables, JSX fix, calibration grouped | Actions: Clean rebuild fixing v18 JSX compilation error; moved Full Year Projection into chart card as two side-by-side tables (Monthly left, Quarterly+Year-End right); created reusable TRow component; calibration section properly grouped by horizon with separate tables per group (weekly/monthly/quarterly) with bold headers instead of parentheses; justification section with all narratives placed between chart and weekly table. | Decisions: Full year breakdown lives inside the chart card now — no separate section. Quarterly table includes Year-End as bold total row at bottom. Clean rebuild preferred over surgical REPLACE when changes touch multiple sections or risk JSX syntax issues.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker Dive v20 — period-specific values in tables | Actions: Used SQL REPLACE surgery to change monthly and quarterly table row computation from cumulative to period-specific (current minus prior period cumulative); monthly shows per-month values (e.g., Apr=780 not 2974), quarterly shows per-quarter values (Q2=2430 not 4624); actuals column also shows period-specific; year-end stays as full-year total; chart remains cumulative. | Decisions: Tables show what happened in each period, chart shows the running total. Period-specific computed client-side by subtracting prior period's cumulative projection. Jan and Q1 show full values since no prior period exists.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker Dive v21 — column reorder + em-dash consistency | Actions: Reordered all table columns to Actual before Predicted across TRow component, monthly/quarterly/weekly headers and bodies; replaced all "pending" and "N/A" with em-dash (\u2014) for consistent empty-state display; applied via 6 targeted SQL REPLACE operations using SET VARIABLE pattern. | Decisions: Actual-first column order across all tables. Em-dash is the universal empty-state indicator — no more mixed "pending"/"N/A" text.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker Dive v21-v22 — column reorder + compilation fix | Actions: Reordered all table columns to Actual before Predicted across TRow component, monthly/quarterly/weekly tables; replaced "pending" and "N/A" with em-dash everywhere; v21 had JSX compilation error from malformed style object in weekly table (`}},fontWeight:` double-brace); fixed in v22 via targeted REPLACE. | Decisions: Actual column comes first in all tables (more natural reading order). Em-dash is the universal placeholder for missing data. Lesson: REPLACE surgery on style objects is fragile — need to verify brace matching after each edit.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker Dive v23 — table divider + justification headers | Actions: Added 1px vertical divider line between monthly and quarterly tables using flex stretch; added color-coded headers to justification section (Revisions red, Monthly purple, Quarterly orange, Year-End red) with spacing between each narrative block. | Decisions: Divider uses alignSelf:"stretch" for full-height span. Justification headers match their section's color identity from the rest of the Dive.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker Dive v24 — justification moved, table gap fixed | Actions: Moved justification section from between chart and weekly table to after weekly table (now: Chart → Monthly/Quarterly tables → Weekly → Justification → Calibration); widened gap between monthly and quarterly side-by-side tables to 24px plus 1px divider with 16px margins for clear visual separation. | Decisions: Justification sits closer to calibration as reference context. Table gap uses both spacing (24px) and divider line for clear break between monthly and quarterly.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker Dive v27 — KPI boxes moved into chart, font sizes increased | Actions: Moved YTD/Month/Quarter/Year KPI boxes from above chart card to inside it (between SVG and monthly/quarterly tables); increased all SVG font sizes (y-axis 10→13, weeks 8→10, months 9→11, quarters 10→12, endpoint labels 9→11). | Decisions: KPI boxes live inside the chart card now — page top is just title + filters + chart. Larger fonts improve readability on the taller 700px chart.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker Dive v28 — KPI boxes finally inside chart, whitespace reduced | Actions: Previous KPI move (v27) failed silently — insertion REPLACE didn't match. Fixed by hiding old KPI div with display:none and inserting new compact KPI boxes (smaller padding/font) between SVG and tables inside chart card; reduced legend marginBottom 8→2px, KPI marginTop/Bottom 8px, tables marginTop 4px. | Decisions: In-chart KPI boxes use tighter styling (20px font, 8px padding) vs the standalone version (22px, 10px). Old KPI div hidden rather than removed to avoid complex multi-line REPLACE failures.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker Dive v30 — weekly before justification | Actions: Swapped Weekly Projections and Justification sections using marker-based extraction and reinsertion; verified order: Weekly (16722) → Justification (18082) → Calibration (18852). | Decisions: Final section order: Chart (with KPI boxes + monthly/quarterly tables) → Weekly Projections → Justification → Calibration.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker Dive v31 — color redesign to neutral palette | Actions: Replaced all decorative colors (purple #8e44ad, orange #e67e22, red #c0392b) with dark navy #1a2744 across section headers, KPI values, table text, chart predicted line/dots, legend, justification sub-headers, calibration horizon headers; kept meaningful colors only (blue for actuals, yellow CI band, green/yellow/red for error/grades). 14 REPLACE operations across two passes. | Decisions: Design language: dark navy (#1a2744) for structure, blue (#3498db) for actuals, navy dashed for predictions, yellow for uncertainty, green/yellow/red only for performance indicators. Color should convey meaning, not decoration.

[2026-04-06] Topic: PS Forecast Tracker Dive — final (v37) + lessons captured | Actions: Created ~/shared/context/intake/motherduck-dive-lessons.md documenting all technical patterns, React/JSX gotchas, data model decisions, and visual design lessons from the 37-version build session. Covers: SET VARIABLE pattern for large pushes, arrow function minification bug, DATE column rendering, SQL surgery fragility, opacity vs background interference, cumulative vs period-specific data model, color-as-meaning design principle, quarter banding, three-layer x-axis. | Decisions: Lessons file goes to intake/ for processing into the body system. Dive is at v37 with final layout: title → filters → chart (with KPI boxes + monthly/quarterly tables inside) → weekly projections → justification → calibration. All Dive IDs documented for reference.

[2026-04-06] Topic: Wiki staging file recovery from Kiro server cache | Actions: Scanned all Kiro server cache snapshots (~/.kiro-server/data/User/globalStorage/) for wiki files not persisted to ~/shared/context/wiki/; recovered 5 files to staging: testing-approach-kate-v5.md (15KB), agent-architecture.md (22KB), oci-playbook-rewrite.md (16KB), workstream-algorithmic-ads-v4/v5.md, workstream-user-experience-v5.md; 16 other files already had equal or larger versions in persistent location. | Decisions: Recovery script takes most recent cache version by timestamp, only copies if dest is missing or smaller. Kate doc lives at ~/shared/context/wiki/staging/testing-approach-kate-v5.md. Ephemeral cache files should be persisted to shared/ during the session that creates them — this was a gap.

[2026-04-06] Topic: Full cache recovery — all markdown files from Kiro server ephemeral cache | Actions: Broad sweep of all .md files (>100 bytes) across all cache snapshots; recovered 33 new files and updated 8 existing files with larger cached versions. Key recoveries: portable-body/ root-level copy (9 files), 15 Karpathy experiment organ variants, 5 intake parallel-test/proposal files, grok-swarm engineer bench file, asana-sync-protocol. Key updates: am-triage.md 7KB→17KB, ieccp-planning-framework 15KB→18KB, testing-approach-kate artifacts version updated. | Decisions: Ephemeral cache is a significant data loss risk — files worked on in sessions but not explicitly saved to shared/ disappear when the cache rotates. Should build a hook or EOD step to sweep cache→shared automatically. The portable-body/ root copy (outside shared/) is a separate concern from shared/portable-body/.

[2026-04-06] Topic: Document organization audit — fragmentation analysis across 526 files | Actions: Scanned all ~/shared/ directories for duplicate content; identified 3 fragmentation types: body system triplication (5 organs × 3 locations with divergent versions), document version sprawl (Kate doc 7+ files, OCI 6+, workstreams 3-4 each, five-year outlook 5+), agent/steering duplication (rw-trainer 4 copies, style guides 2 each); created ~/shared/context/intake/document-organization-audit.md with full analysis, proposed lifecycle (Intake→Research→Draft→Review→Staged→Published→Archive), directory mapping, and 6 immediate cleanup actions. | Decisions: shared/context/body/ is source of truth for organs; portable-body/ is generated not authored; artifacts/ holds first drafts only — wiki staging becomes canonical once pipeline starts; ~/portable-body/ root copy should be deleted; document registry needed (DuckDB or markdown index). Awaiting Richard's approval before executing cleanup.

[2026-04-06] Topic: Document registry built in MotherDuck + agent steering | Actions: Created ps_analytics.docs schema with 3 tables (documents, versions, file_index) and 3 views (canonical, duplicates, unregistered); seeded 19 documents with canonical paths and stages, 30 file_index entries mapping duplicates/prior-versions/reviews to their doc_id; created auto-included steering file (shared/.kiro/steering/document-registry.md) instructing all agents to query registry before creating/editing files, with SQL examples for lookup, registration, and stage transitions. | Decisions: Files stay as markdown on disk, DuckDB is the coordination/index layer. Steering file is auto-included so every agent session sees it. 22 archive-candidate files identified (13 for Kate doc alone). ~500 files still unregistered — bulk registration is a follow-up task. Registry replaces the scattered directory-based organization with a queryable single source of truth.

[2026-04-06] Topic: Full document registry — bulk registration + DOC stamps on all files | Actions: Built Python scripts (export-registry.py, stamp_docs.sh) to scan all 526 .md files, generate doc_ids, categorize by directory, export as CSV; loaded via DuckDB read_csv into ps_analytics.docs (475 documents, 531 file_index entries, 56 duplicates identified); stamped 466 canonical files with `<!-- DOC-XXXX | doc-id -->` HTML comment on line 1 as machine-readable library identifier. | Decisions: DOC numbers (DOC-0001 through DOC-0475) are the universal identifier — visible in file, queryable in DuckDB, linkable to Asana. HTML comment format chosen so stamps don't render in markdown but are readable by agents. CSV import via read_csv was the solution for bulk loading (150KB SQL file was too large for MCP tool). notes column stores doc_num until schema is cleaned up.

[2026-04-06] Topic: Document registry discoverability — tags + quick-reference table | Actions: Added tags column to docs.documents, tagged 19 key documents with searchable keywords (e.g., kate→'kate,testing,skip-level,methodology'); rewrote steering file with quick-reference table of 12 most-used documents (DOC number + path), tag-aware search examples, and clearer instructions for creating/stamping new files. | Decisions: Three discovery layers: (1) quick-reference table in steering for common lookups without DuckDB, (2) tag search for natural language queries, (3) title/doc_id search for everything else. Tags added incrementally — 19 key docs tagged now, rest findable by title.

[2026-04-06] Topic: Document registry rebuild — duck_id as primary key with descriptive slugs | Actions: Rebuilt entire registry with `duck_id` as primary key using `category-topic-detail` naming pattern (e.g., organ-brain, strategy-aeo-ai-overviews-pov, wiki-testing-approach-kate); regenerated 510 documents + 531 file_index entries via Python script; re-stamped all files with `<!-- DOC-XXXX | duck_id: slug -->` format; rebuilt DuckDB schema (documents/file_index/versions tables + canonical/duplicates views); updated steering file with duck_id-based search examples and quick-reference table. | Decisions: duck_id replaces doc_id as the universal identifier — designed to be guessable from topic so agents can search without knowing exact vocabulary. Tags column retained but less critical now that duck_id itself is searchable. File stamp format includes both DOC number and duck_id for dual lookup.

[2026-04-06] Topic: Document location analysis — artifacts vs wiki vs context overlap | Actions: Queried file_index for cross-location duplicates; found 19 same-duck_id duplicates (mostly portable-body copies) but zero cross-location (artifacts↔wiki↔context) overlaps in registry — because duck_id generation collapsed them. Identified the real problem: when artifacts enter wiki pipeline, copies diverge (artifact stays stale, wiki staging becomes current). | Decisions: Proposed `shared/docs/` as single home for all publishable content — stage tracked in DuckDB not directory structure. System files (organs, protocols, experiments) stay in context/. Migration would consolidate artifacts/ + wiki/staging/ into docs/, update DuckDB paths, re-stamp. Awaiting Richard's approval.

[2026-04-06] Topic: Full document sprawl map — discovered ~/artifacts/ mirror and ~/wiki/ ghost | Actions: Listed ~/artifacts/ (full mirror of shared/artifacts/ from agent-bridge git repo, 63+ files) and ~/wiki/ (empty directory); mapped complete sprawl: 3 copies of artifacts (shared/artifacts, ~/artifacts, wiki staging versions), 3 copies of body organs (shared/context/body, shared/portable-body, ~/portable-body), plus empty ~/wiki/ ghost. | Decisions: Confirmed shared/docs/ consolidation plan is the right move. ~/artifacts/ stays as git repo checkout but bridge-sync should read from shared/docs/. ~/wiki/ and ~/portable-body/ should be deleted. Migration scoped as potential dedicated session — too large for end-of-day.

[2026-04-06] Topic: shared/wiki/ structure proposal — full directory layout + migration plan | Actions: Queried DuckDB for complete category/subcategory/stage breakdown (39 groups, 510 docs); designed 10-directory structure (strategy, testing, markets, operations, reporting, callouts, meetings, research, reviews, archive + _meta); created detailed proposal at shared/context/intake/wiki-structure-proposal.md with directory layout, duck_id prefix mapping, DuckDB integration patterns, migration source→destination table (~260 files), and cleanup list (5 directories to delete post-migration). | Decisions: Directories organized by content type not pipeline stage — DuckDB tracks stage. duck_id prefix maps 1:1 to directory. System files (organs, protocols, experiments) stay in shared/context/. Callouts get market subdirectories due to volume (101 files). Awaiting Richard's approval before executing migration.

[2026-04-07] Topic: shared/wiki/ migration executed — 280 files moved, registry updated | Actions: Created shared/wiki/ directory structure (10 topic dirs + 11 callout market subdirs); built and ran migration script copying 280 files from shared/artifacts/, shared/context/wiki/, shared/context/meetings/, shared/context/active/callouts/, shared/research/ into shared/wiki/{topic}/; regenerated DuckDB registry with 277 updated canonical paths pointing to shared/wiki/; re-stamped 502 files with duck_id; updated steering file with new directory layout and paths. | Decisions: Copy-not-move strategy — old locations preserved until Richard confirms. Old directories (shared/artifacts/, shared/context/wiki/, ~/artifacts/, ~/wiki/, ~/portable-body/) pending cleanup after verification. Callouts organized by market subdirectory (101 files across 11 markets). System files (organs, protocols, experiments) stay in shared/context/.

[2026-04-07] Topic: Post-migration cleanup — old document locations deleted | Actions: Removed 8 directories: ~/wiki/ (empty ghost), ~/portable-body/ (cache copy), ~/artifacts/ (git mirror), shared/artifacts/ (replaced by shared/wiki/), shared/context/wiki/ (replaced), shared/context/meetings/ (moved), shared/context/active/callouts/ (moved), shared/research/ (moved). Verified all gone + 280 files healthy in shared/wiki/. | Decisions: Cleanup is permanent — shared/wiki/ is now the single source of truth for all publishable content. System files remain in shared/context/. Bridge-sync will need updating to read from shared/wiki/ instead of shared/artifacts/.

[2026-04-07] Topic: Home directory cleanup — symlinks, stale files, backup dirs | Actions: Identified 8 symlinks in ~/ pointing into shared/ (created Mar 31 as shortcuts, 2 now broken from migration), plus kiro-steering-backup/, aui-flow-mcp-log/, and asana_enrichment_req.json; removed all of them. Home directory now contains only shared/ and brazil-pkg-cache (system). | Decisions: No more symlinks from ~/ into shared/ — agents and users navigate through shared/ directly. Steering backup no longer needed (steering files are in shared/.kiro/steering/). Home directory should stay clean — only shared/ matters.

[2026-04-07] Topic: shared/ directory cleanup — temp files removed, structure simplified | Actions: Removed ~40 temp files (registry CSVs, migration scripts, batch SQL, JSON dumps, dive working files from tools/data/dashboards); merged audit-reports/ into context/archive/; removed DIRECTORY-MAP.md (replaced by DuckDB registry). Final shared/ has 8 directories: wiki/ (your docs), uploads/ (drop zone), dashboards/ (HTML), context/ (agent infra), tools/ (scripts), data/ (DuckDB/exports), portable-body/ (bridge), credentials/ (auth). | Decisions: Top-level shared/ optimized for human browsing — publishable content (wiki/) and drop zone (uploads/) are prominent, agent infrastructure (context/tools/data/) is clearly separate. Temp files from today's registry/migration work cleaned up immediately rather than accumulating.

[2026-04-07] Topic: shared/ directory consolidation — merges, empties removed, tools organized | Actions: Merged context/tools/ into tools/, context/archive/ into wiki/archive/, moved context/changelog.md into context/body/; deleted empty data/ subdirs (markets/raw/testing), orphaned tools/wbr-auto-pipeline, empty tools/autonomous and tools/team; organized 19 loose Python scripts in tools/ into tools/asana/ (10), tools/comms/ (7), tools/routines/ (1), tools/scripts/ (4). | Decisions: No more duplicate archive or tools directories. Loose scripts grouped by function (asana sync, comms/email/hedy, routines). Final shared/ has 6 clean top-level directories with no loose files at any root level.

[2026-04-07] Topic: Level-3 cleanup — state files separated, duplicates removed, archive flattened | Actions: Moved 14 JSON/JSONL/TSV/log state files from context/active/ to new data/state/ directory; removed 4 duplicate/empty files from wiki/strategy/ (0-byte research brief, duplicate agent-architecture, superseded five-year-outlook, duplicate research brief); flattened wiki/archive/ from 5 nested subdirs to flat; removed context/body/changelog-system.md duplicate, context/protocols/.bak file, tools/data/ps-analytics.duckdb duplicate. | Decisions: context/active/ is now markdown-only (protocols + docs). State files live in data/state/ alongside data/duckdb and data/exports. Archive is flat — no nested subdirectories.

[2026-04-07] Topic: Level-4 cleanup — prior versions archived, duplicates removed | Actions: Reviewed full 4-level tree; removed 2 unprefixed change-log.md duplicates from DE/UK callout dirs; archived 3 prior versions (testing-approach-kate v1, workstream-algorithmic-ads v4, five-year-outlook v1) to wiki/archive/; removed 0-byte ps-five-year-outlook-research-brief.md. Active wiki directories now contain only canonical (latest) versions. | Decisions: Prior versions go to wiki/archive/ rather than being deleted — preserves history. Only the latest versioned file stays in the active directory. 0-byte files are deleted outright.

[2026-04-07] Topic: Broken reference audit after migration — significant breakage found | Actions: Searched all active system files (body organs, protocols, agents, hooks, skills, steering, tools) for references to deleted/moved paths; found ~80 broken references across 12 path patterns in critical files: body organs (8 files), wiki agents (6 agents + configs), callout agents (3), bridge sync (hook+skill+agent), eod-system-refresh protocol, sharepoint-sync, wiki-asana-sync, send_brief. Session-log and intake files also have historical refs but those are non-breaking. | Decisions: Fix is mechanical (path substitution) but touches core system files — needs a systematic find-and-replace script rather than manual edits. Scoped for next session. Key substitutions needed: shared/artifacts/ → shared/wiki/, shared/context/wiki/ → shared/wiki/, shared/context/meetings/ → shared/wiki/meetings/, shared/context/active/callouts/ → shared/wiki/callouts/, shared/research/ → shared/wiki/research/, portable-body/ → removed (steering note only), context/tools/ → tools/, context/archive/ → wiki/archive/.

[2026-04-07] Topic: Broken reference fix — 52 files updated with new paths | Actions: Built and ran fix-broken-refs.sh script with path substitution map (shared/artifacts/→shared/wiki/, shared/context/wiki/→shared/wiki/, shared/context/meetings/→shared/wiki/meetings/, shared/context/active/callouts/→shared/wiki/callouts/, shared/research/→shared/wiki/research/, shared/portable-body/→shared/context/, context/tools/→tools/, context/archive/→wiki/archive/, context/changelog.md→context/body/changelog.md); fixed 52 active system files across body organs (8), protocols (3), wiki agents+configs (14), callout agents (3), agent system files (3), skills (3), steering (3), tools (8), wiki/_meta (7). Fixed conceptual portable-body references in bridge-sync hook/skill/agents. | Decisions: Historical references in session-log/intake/experiments/specs intentionally skipped — they're accurate records of what existed at the time. Repo now reflects shared/wiki/ as the single content home with all old paths removed.

[2026-04-07] Topic: Loop page ingestion into DuckDB + AM-Backend integration | Actions: Created docs.loop_pages table in DuckDB, ingested all 4 Loop pages (Brandon 1:1, DDD, MBR/QBR, Weekly Meeting) via sharepoint_read_loop with full content + previews, wrote loop-page-sync.md protocol, added Subagent D to am-backend-parallel.md (parallel with Slack/Asana/Email), updated am-auto hook to fire 5 subagents, updated resource isolation table. Also tested a 5th Loop URL (empty working session template — skipped). | Decisions: Loop pages stored in DuckDB not steering files; auto-refresh on >12h staleness; new pages added by INSERT into docs.loop_pages — next AM run picks them up automatically.

[2026-04-07] Topic: Timezone configuration for agent/shell | Actions: Checked TZ env var and .bashrc (neither set, system running UTC). Explained two-layer problem: shell time vs agent prompt time. | Decisions: Steering confirmed as the structural solution for agent timezone awareness; TZ env var offered for shell-level fix. No changes made — awaiting direction on .bashrc edit and hook failure details.

[2026-04-07] Topic: Pacific timezone fix for all daily hooks | Actions: Added TZ=America/Los_Angeles to .bashrc; injected timezone instructions + runtime date command into all 4 daily hooks (am-auto, am-triage, eod-meeting-sync, eod-refresh) | Decisions: Three-layer structural fix (env var + hook prompt injection + runtime verification) chosen over steering-only. Root cause: platform injects UTC in current_date_and_time, causing EOD to think its next-day morning and AM to misreport time.

[2026-04-07] Topic: Karpathy experiment CLI discovery + attempted execution | Actions: Located karpathy-loop.sh at ~/shared/tools/scripts/ (not ~/shared/tools/), verified kiro-cli binary + karpathy agent + eval-a/b/c agents all present, checked DuckDB experiment count (110 total, 92 keeps, 23 reverts), launched background batch targeting 125, killed after rate limit hits. Fixed path in eod-backend.md protocol. Updated eod-experiments.json with findings. | Decisions: Karpathy experiments must run standalone (no active chat session) to avoid API rate limit conflicts. Path reference corrected in protocol.

[2026-04-07] Topic: Pacific timezone fix for all daily hooks | Actions: Added TZ=America/Los_Angeles to .bashrc; injected timezone instructions + runtime date command into all 4 daily hooks (am-auto, am-triage, eod-meeting-sync, eod-refresh). Discovered session-log.md lives at /shared/user/context/intake/ not ~/shared/context/intake/. | Decisions: Three-layer structural fix (env var + hook prompt injection + runtime verification) chosen over steering-only. Root cause: platform injects UTC in current_date_and_time, causing EOD hooks to think its next-day morning when triggered at Pacific evening.

[2026-04-07] Topic: Karpathy experiments successful execution | Actions: Relaunched karpathy-loop.sh (rate limit was external conflict, not systemic). Batch of 10 experiments completed in 26 min — 8 KEEP, 2 REVERT. Targets: nervous-system, spine, amcc, brain, richard-style-email, richard-style-mbr, device, gut, richard-style-slack, richard-style-docs. Biggest win: gut×MERGE -195w zero accuracy loss. Updated eod-experiments.json with full results. Total experiments now 129. | Decisions: REMOVE anti-pattern on unique content confirmed 9/9 reverted. SPLIT safest technique (5/5). 80% keep rate flagged as above healthy 50% target — next batch needs more exploration.
[2026-04-07] Topic: Cross-Environment Inventory Sync design review | Actions: Read body.md, spine.md, hands.md, device.md, sync.sh, agent-bridge repo structure, ops schema in DuckDB — full context load to evaluate design against existing infra. Delivered 8-point review with implementation order. | Decisions: Design approved with caveats: kiro-sync.py doesn't exist (clarify build-vs-existing), transfer latency is acceptable but should be documented, generateDescription needs spec, Downloads dir is noise (subtraction), scannable conflates two concepts, needs staleness TTL on inventory rows, orphaned transfer_requests need handling. Suggested starting with DuckDB tables + SSH scanner from current env.
[2026-04-07] Topic: Inventory Sync deep review (round 2) | Actions: Loaded ALL organs (heart, brain, gut, nervous-system, memory, amcc, hands, device, spine, body), ops schema columns, data_freshness table (17 sources), agent-bridge repo structure (uploads/sheets/ with 20 files, context/intake/ with 33 files), .gitignore, sync.sh, bridge-sync skill. Wrote 298-line review doc (inventory-sync-review.md) to agent-bridge intake with 15 numbered findings + 5 beyond-scope ideas. | Decisions: Design approved with integration gaps: use existing uploads/ dir (not Documents/Downloads), integrate with ops.data_freshness (don't reinvent staleness), embed SSH hook in EOD-2 cascade (not standalone), expand category enum to match actual dirs, add consumption-pattern views for daily hooks, cache SHA-256 by mtime, template-based descriptions, map transfer priority to task priority, manifests as cold-start source of truth. Implementation order: DuckDB tables → SSH scanner → EOD-2 integration → local scanner (separate session).

[2026-04-07] Topic: MCP server connectivity audit | Actions: Read user-level and workspace-level mcp.json configs, cross-referenced defined servers against available tools in session. | Decisions: 4 servers identified as enabled-but-not-connected (ai-community-slack-mcp, amazon-sharepoint-mcp, knowledge-discovery-mcp, xwiki-mcp) — likely binary-not-on-PATH issues. 6 intentionally disabled servers confirmed. 8 servers confirmed connected.

[2026-04-07] Topic: MCP server connectivity diagnosis (deep dive) | Actions: Ran `which` on 4 non-connected servers (all not found), inspected ~/.aim/mcp-servers/ and ~/.toolbox/bin/, checked all MCP-related symlinks for broken targets. | Decisions: amazon-sharepoint-mcp has broken symlink (AIM install incomplete, needs reinstall). xwiki-mcp, knowledge-discovery-mcp, ai-community-slack-mcp were never installed on this DevSpaces instance. Awaiting Richard's call on disabling vs reinstalling.

[2026-04-07] Topic: MCP reinstall + hook dependency check | Actions: Audited all MCP-related directories (toolbox, aim, mcp-registry). Installed 3 of 4 missing MCPs via mcp-registry (knowledge-discovery-mcp, ai-community-slack-mcp, amazon-sharepoint-mcp). xwiki-mcp failed — package owner's version set is deprecated/broken. Checked all 16 hooks for dependencies on the 4 problematic MCPs. | Decisions: Only sharepoint-sync.kiro.hook references sharepoint, but it calls a Python CLI not the MCP directly — no hook blockers. Installs landed but binaries not yet on PATH (mcp-registry installed to a location outside current PATH; sharepoint symlink still broken pointing to ~/.aim/mcp-servers/). xwiki-mcp needs owner intervention or alternative package. Reconnect from MCP Server panel to test.

[2026-04-07] Topic: AM-Backend Recovery Run (Phase 1 + Phase 5) | Actions: Recovered 3 failed subagents (Slack A, Asana B1, Asana B2) from cancelled/403'd morning run. 114 tasks UPSERTed to asana_tasks, 137 snapshot rows, 8 Slack signals ingested. Updated data freshness + l1_streak. Email/Calendar/Loop already landed from prior run. | Decisions: Skipped Subagent C (email) and D (loop) since DuckDB showed today's data. Phases 2-4 (processing) deferred — ingestion recovery was priority.

[2026-04-07] Topic: Em-dash ban added to all writing style guides | Actions: Updated 14 files (7 style guides × 2 copies in .kiro/steering/ and shared/.kiro/steering/) — added "no em-dashes" rule to core voice (Things Richard Does NOT Do + Drafting Checklist #11), email (new Failure #3), Slack (What NOT to Do list), WBR/MBR/docs (new Common Failure #4 each), Amazon norms (new subsection before Cut anything duplicative). | Decisions: Rule applies to drafted output only, not to the style guides' own instructional prose. Every guide now includes both the prohibition and a scan-and-replace check step.

[2026-04-07] Topic: Full WBR callout pipeline W14 — ingest through blind review | Actions: Fixed ingester _detect_anomalies method conflict, ran ingester on W14 xlsx (all 10 markets), generated data briefs + callout drafts, wrote 8 callouts (AU/MX/US/CA/JP/UK/DE/ES), consolidated EU5 into single callout, updated SKILL.md with: no confidence gate (all markets get callouts), EU5 consolidation rule, two-lens blind review rubric (Lens 1: Richard's Voice — attribution/structure/voice/economy; Lens 2: Brandon's Read — so-what/ownership/accuracy/strategic), 8/10 combined threshold with 6-floor per dimension. | Decisions: Dropped 66% confidence gate — every market gets a callout regardless of signal strength. EU5 consolidated into one 150-word block. Blind review upgraded from generic 5-dimension to two-lens (Richard style + Brandon reading) with 8/10 bar matching wiki-critic pattern. FR/IT initially excluded, then included per Richard's direction.

[2026-04-07] Topic: WBR callout pipeline refinements — consistency check + score logging | Actions: Created ps.callout_scores table (8 dimensions, attempt tracking, consistency flags), ps.callout_calibration view (per-market averages, weakest dimensions), ps.callout_trend view (weekly quality trajectory); added Step 6 (cross-callout consistency check for contradictions) and Step 7 (mandatory DuckDB score logging) to SKILL.md; confirmed per-unit scoring (not aggregate). | Decisions: Every pipeline run logs scores to DuckDB — non-negotiable. Consistency check catches cross-callout contradictions that per-unit scoring misses. Calibration views enable rubric adjustment over time as data accumulates.

[2026-04-08] Topic: WBR data pipeline — full xlsx ingestion into MotherDuck wide table | Actions: Created ps.performance wide table (one row per market × period with all KPIs: regs/cost/cpa/clicks/impressions/cpc/cvr/ctr + brand/nb splits + ieccp). Loaded 1,511 weekly rows (170 weeks for 8 markets, 43 AU, 108 MX), 10,572 daily rows (all 10 markets), 40 monthly spend rows (Jan-Apr 2026). Loaded OP2 spend targets into ps.targets for all 10 markets × 12 months. Fixed ingester _detect_anomalies method conflict. Extracted full 208-week history from xlsx (not just 52). | Decisions: Replaced EAV ps.metrics pattern with wide ps.performance table — one row = all KPIs for a market-period. OP2 regs targets not available in xlsx (only spend OP2 found). Projection step for callout pipeline still pending — needs Bayesian engine wired to new table.

[2026-04-08] Topic: WBR projection system audit — existing forecast infrastructure + gap analysis | Actions: Audited ps.forecasts (75 rows, AU/MX only), ps.cumulative_projections (59 rows), ps.forecast_revisions (75 rows with drift tracking), ps.forecast_narratives (10 rows), forecast_accuracy view (AU 75% hit rate, MX 80%), dive_forecast views; read Bayesian engine (core.py, engine.py, models.py, types.py); read ingester _project_month and _read_ieccp; read eyes.md for ie%CCP context; checked IECCP tab (104 weeks of history available). | Decisions: Identified 5 gaps: (1) no Brand/NB split in forecasts, (2) no ie%CCP integration in projections, (3) only AU/MX have forecasts (8 markets missing), (4) no W15 next-week projections, (5) no % to OP2 regs (spend OP2 loaded, regs OP2 not found in xlsx). Brand/NB split is critical — Brand tied to seasonality/events, NB tied to spend management/ie%CCP. Building the extended projection engine scoped as next focused session.

[2026-04-08] Topic: WBR projection engine built — Brand/NB split with market strategies | Actions: Built shared/tools/prediction/project.py with 4 market strategy profiles (AU=efficiency/hit OP2, MX=ie%CCP bound at 100%, JP=brand-dominant, others=balanced 50-65% ie%CCP). Loaded 594 ie%CCP values (66 weeks × 9 markets) into ps.performance. Generated W15/M04/Q2 projections for all 10 markets with Brand/NB splits, wrote 264 forecast rows to ps.forecasts + revision tracking. Projection tables written to callouts/projections-w14.md. | Decisions: Brand modeled with YoY seasonality (40% weight), NB modeled with ie%CCP constraint and spend-management logic. Total = Brand + NB bottom-up. JP NB too small to model (~7 regs/wk), treated as constant. AU has no ie%CCP tracking. MX ie%CCP at 126% triggers NB spend pullback in projection.

[2026-04-08] Topic: WBR pipeline integration audit + gap fixes | Actions: Audited full pipeline for end-to-end readiness; identified 4 gaps (ingester→MotherDuck sync, projection not in skill, prediction scoring not automated, no orchestration wrapper); added steps 9-12 to SKILL.md (score predictions, generate projections, sync to MotherDuck, present with projections); added score_predictions() and sync_to_motherduck() functions to project.py; updated End-to-End Weekly Sequence in SKILL.md notes. | Decisions: Pipeline is documented and functional but not yet single-trigger automated. Ingester + sync + score + project + callout are separate steps that need a thin orchestration wrapper. All data flows through ps.performance (wide table) and ps.forecasts (predictions with scoring). Prediction feedback loop closes each week when actuals score prior forecasts.

[2026-04-08] Topic: WBR orchestration wrapper + MotherDuck dive view fixes | Actions: Built wbr-pipeline.sh (single command: ingest → sync → score → project); fixed dive_forecast_combined (added missing market/metric_name columns); rebuilt dive_forecast_timeline to use ps.performance instead of old ps.metrics; created dive_forecast_summary (human-readable with status icons), dive_forecast_chart (actuals+forecasts side-by-side), rebuilt dive_unified_timeline (actuals→forecast transition with CI bands and Brand/NB splits); cleaned 12 duplicate revision rows. | Decisions: Pipeline is now single-command via wbr-pipeline.sh. All dive views read from ps.performance (wide table). dive_unified_timeline is the primary chart view — shows actual line transitioning to forecast line with CI band at the current week boundary.

[2026-04-08] Topic: MotherDuck PS Forecast Tracker dive critique (MX filter) | Actions: Reviewed 4 screenshots of the dive UI; identified 10 issues — cumulative/weekly mismatch on chart and weekly table (actuals showing cumulative ~23K instead of weekly ~297), stale KPI cards (showing Jan instead of April, Q1 instead of Q2), inverted CI display (1,034–674), W1-W8 actuals missing despite data existing in ps.performance, error percentages nonsensical (90%+) due to comparing weekly predictions against cumulative actuals. | Decisions: Root cause is the dive mixing ps.cumulative_projections (cumulative) with ps.forecasts (weekly). Views need to consistently source from ps.performance for actuals and ps.forecasts for predictions. KPI cards need to show current period, not first prediction.

[2026-04-08] Topic: MotherDuck dive views rebuilt for 2026 forecast tracker | Actions: Created 4 new dive views — dive_weekly (main chart+table, 2026 W1-W52 actuals+forecasts), dive_monthly (12 months with OP2), dive_quarterly (Q1-Q4 with OP2), dive_kpi_cards (YTD/current month/quarter/year-end). Fixed period_key format mismatch (W09→W9). Rebuilt dive_unified_timeline as alias for dive_weekly. Updated dive_forecast_combined with market+metric columns. All views now read from ps.performance (actuals) and ps.forecasts (predictions), not cumulative_projections. | Decisions: Dive is 2026-only. Weekly data is non-cumulative (actual weekly regs, not YTD running total). Old cumulative_projections table no longer feeds any dive view. Scored weeks (W9/W10/W11/W14) show HIT/MISS status. W15 shows forecast with Brand/NB split and CI band.

[2026-04-08] Topic: MotherDuck dive views — full rebuild of all data sources | Actions: Rebuilt all dive views in-place (same names, new queries): dive_forecast_scorecard (2026-only, from ps.forecasts), dive_forecast_combined (added market/metric/method), dive_forecast_chart (from dive_weekly), dive_forecast_kpis (2026-only accuracy), cumulative_projections (reloaded 153 rows from ps.forecasts). Computed monthly actuals from weekly rollup into ps.performance (40 rows). Fixed W1 Dec→Jan assignment using Thursday rule. Fixed W09/W9 period_key format mismatch. Created cumulative_projections_v2 view with actual→forecast transition. | Decisions: All dive views now source from ps.performance (actuals) and ps.forecasts (predictions). Monthly actuals computed from weekly rollup using Thursday-of-week month assignment. Dive should auto-reflect changes since view names are unchanged.

[2026-04-08] Topic: Full-year Bayesian projections loaded into MotherDuck for dive | Actions: Renamed prediction/types.py→ptypes.py (was shadowing Python stdlib), updated 8 import references. Built project_full.py with Brand/NB split Bayesian projections for W15-W52 × 10 markets. Loaded 3,252 rows into cumulative_projections (weekly/monthly/quarterly/year_end × regs/cost/cpa × 10 markets + WW + EU5). Fixed quarterly values from standalone to cumulative (Q4=Dec=YE). Rebuilt cumulative_projections table without PK constraint. Rebuilt cumulative_actuals view to source from ps.performance with WW+EU5 aggregates. | Decisions: All projections are internally consistent: weeks sum to months, months sum to quarters, Q4=Dec=YE. Component filter buttons need updating from [MX,AU,PA] to all 12 entities. Hardcoded actCum values in React component should be removed (data now comes from queries). Market strategies applied: AU=efficiency, MX=ie%CCP bound (0.79 adj), JP=brand-dominant, others=balanced.

[2026-04-08] Topic: Projection engine fix — median + dampened trend replaces mean + linear extrapolation | Actions: Rebuilt all projections using median (not mean) as base, dampened trend slope (capped at ±5% of median per week), 30% floor (never project below 30% of median). Fixed AU from collapsing to 35 regs/wk → now 232 W15, flooring at 73. Fixed quarterly values to be cumulative (Q4=Dec=YE). Forced WW/EU5 consistency. Renamed prediction/types.py→ptypes.py to fix Python stdlib shadow. Loaded 3,028 rows for all 12 entities. | Decisions: Median is more robust than mean for markets with recent downtrend outliers (AU). Trend dampening prevents runaway extrapolation. 30% floor prevents nonsensical near-zero projections. React component filter buttons still need updating to show all 12 markets.

[2026-04-08] Topic: Projection engine fix — median replaces mean, AU test case | Actions: Diagnosed AU projection collapse (112→35 regs/wk due to OLS trend extrapolating 8-week decline). Switched from mean+trend to median-based estimates for all markets (median resistant to outliers and short-term trends). Rebuilt all 3,203 projection rows with median + IQR-based CIs + seasonal indices + ie%CCP constraints. AU W15 now 244 [200-288] vs old 112. Fixed WW/EU5 Q4/YE consistency. Dropped PK constraint on cumulative_projections. Renamed prediction/types.py→ptypes.py (stdlib shadow fix). | Decisions: Median is the default baseline for all projections — more stable than mean+OLS for markets with short history or volatile recent trends. IQR replaces std dev for CI width (robust to outliers). Seasonal index still applied when 52+ weeks available. React component filter buttons still need updating to show all 12 entities.

[2026-04-08] Topic: W14 callout output directory drift | Actions: Moved all W14 callout files (10 markets + WW summaries) from context/active/callouts/ to wiki/callouts/ to match W12/W13 pattern. Updated wbr-callouts hook prompt to explicitly specify output directory (wiki/callouts/). Removed misplaced context/active/callouts/ directory. | Decisions: wiki/callouts/ is the canonical callout archive. Hook now enforces this path to prevent future drift.

[2026-04-08] Topic: Clean projection rebuild — fixed duplicates, AU test case verified | Actions: Dropped and recreated cumulative_projections table (clean slate). Single-pass projection for all 10 markets + WW + EU5 using median-based estimates with seasonal indices. Fixed duplicate rows (old runs left residual data). AU now projects 244 regs/wk (median) with seasonal variation (315 in W30, 151 in W52). All 10 markets YE=Dec=Q4 consistent. 1,524 total rows, zero duplicates. | Decisions: Table recreated without PK to avoid constraint issues. Single-pass insert eliminates dedup need. AU YE projection: 15,396 regs (was 4,195 in broken run, 12,697 in dampened-trend run). Seasonal indices from 170-week history now properly applied for markets with 52+ weeks.

[2026-04-08] Topic: W14 callout rewrites (MX projection, AU word count, OP2 removal) | Actions: Projected MX full week using W11-W13 Fri/Sat day-of-week patterns (303 regs, -14% WoW vs misleading 238/-33% from partial data). Tightened AU callout from 150→110 words. Stripped "vs OP2 Spend" column from all projection tables. Updated standalone files, consolidated WBR callouts, and projections-w14.md. Relocated W14 outputs from context/active/callouts/ to wiki/callouts/ and updated hook prompt to enforce output directory. | Decisions: wiki/callouts/ is canonical callout archive. MX partial-week data must be projected before callout writing. No OP2 percentage references in projection tables.

[2026-04-08] Topic: WBR callout formatting rules + style guide update | Actions: Added Formatting Rules section to WBR callout guide (word count 110±10, arrow notation for volume changes inline not parenthesized, plain week references without parens, no OP2 in projections). Fixed AU/MX callouts and consolidated WBR file to match new rules. Trimmed MX prose from 124→108 words. | Decisions: Volume changes use inline arrow notation (110→74 not (110→74)). Week labels are plain text (W7 not (W7)). Parentheses reserved for percentage changes and dollar amounts only.

[2026-04-08] Topic: Standalone projection tables replace cumulative — fixed AU 2025 data leak | Actions: Created ps.weekly_actuals (336 rows) and ps.projections_standalone (1,524 rows) with standalone (non-cumulative) values. Fixed AU bug where 2025-W24 through 2025-W52 history was being treated as 2026 actuals (Jul-Dec showing as "actual" when they were 2025 data). Now only 2026 weeks used for actuals, all history used for model building. All 10 markets consistent (months=quarters=YE). AU monthly: Jan 975, Feb 1,079, Mar 993 (actuals), Apr-Dec forecasts. | Decisions: Standalone values are simpler — chart computes cumulative on the fly, tables show standalone directly. Old cumulative_projections and cumulative_actuals dropped. React component needs updating to read from new tables.

[2026-04-08] Topic: Callout documentation audit | Actions: Audited all callout writing guidance across shared/. Found 5 locations with overlapping/conflicting rules: steering file (richard-style-wbr.md), wiki guide (wbr-callout-guide.md), callout-writer.md agent def, callout-reviewer.md agent def, and a dead placeholder (callout-principles.md that references itself). Synced formatting rules (word count 110±10, no raw volume numbers, no OP2 in projections) to both steering file and wiki guide. | Decisions: Need to consolidate to single source of truth — pending Richard's call on whether steering file or wiki guide is canonical. callout-principles.md placeholder needs to either be populated or deleted.

[2026-04-08] Topic: Callout documentation consolidation | Actions: Established callout-writer.md as single source of truth for all callout writing rules. Updated wiki guide, steering file (richard-style-wbr.md), and callout-principles.md placeholder to reference it instead of duplicating rules. Added hook invocation note to all three docs — callout-writer/reviewer should be invoked via the pipeline hook, not directly. Removed raw volume numbers from AU/MX callouts, trimmed MX to 108 words. | Decisions: callout-writer.md is canonical. All other callout docs reference it. Pipeline hook is the invocation path.

[2026-04-08] Topic: Backward-compatible views for dive component | Actions: Created cumulative_actuals view (computes YTD/QTD cumulative from weekly_actuals) and cumulative_projections view (computes running cumulative from projections_standalone for monthly/quarterly, passes through for weekly/year_end/CPA). Both use the old table names so the existing React component works without code changes. | Decisions: Views are the compatibility layer — standalone tables are the source of truth, cumulative is computed on the fly. Component still needs filter button updates for all markets.

[2026-04-08] Topic: AU/MX W14 callout review against callout-writer rules | Actions: Ran manual review of AU and MX callouts against callout-writer.md spec. Fixed em-dashes in both Notes. Rewrote AU Note to be forward-looking. Added missing W15 watch and W15 optimization supplementary sections to both markets. Updated consolidated WBR file. | Decisions: Reviewer can run as subagent (no agent invocations needed, just read/write/DuckDB). For 2-market reviews, inline review is faster than spinning up the full pipeline.

[2026-04-08] Topic: OP2 reference rule clarification in callouts | Actions: Added vs OP2 spend to AU (-14%) and MX (+67%) monthly close lines in callouts and consolidated WBR file. Updated callout-writer.md, steering file, and wiki guide to clarify: OP2 comparison required in prose headline monthly projection line, but not in projection tables. | Decisions: OP2 reference always appears in the monthly close/projection sentence within callout prose. Projection tables remain clean (Regs, Spend, CPA, ie%CCP only).

[2026-04-08] Topic: Dive component rewrite — standalone values, no hardcoded actuals | Actions: Wrote dive-v2.jsx that reads from ps.weekly_actuals + ps.projections_standalone (standalone values). Eliminated hardcoded actCum, cumulative math in tables, and old table references. Chart computes cumulative on the fly. All 12 market filters (AU/MX/US/CA/UK/DE/FR/IT/ES/JP/EU5/WW). Monthly/quarterly/weekly tables show standalone values directly. KPI cards show current month (Apr), quarter (Q2), year-end. Also created backward-compatible cumulative_actuals and cumulative_projections views for the old component. | Decisions: Component needs to be copy-pasted into MotherDuck dive editor. Standalone is the source of truth — cumulative only computed for the chart line.

[2026-04-08] Topic: OP2 registration targets missing from DuckDB | Actions: Traced OP2 format through previous callouts (W12/W13 AU/MX). Reformatted W14 callout monthly close lines to match Richard's style: parenthesized "(vs. OP2: -14% spend)" for AU, inline "ahead of OP2 (+67% spend)" for MX. Updated steering file headline template. Discovered ingester reads OP2 regs from xlsx but the projections table schema doesn't match the INSERT columns — op2_regs, op2_spend, vs_op2_regs_pct silently fail. ps.targets only has cost rows (manual op2_q2_update load). | Decisions: Ingester schema mismatch is a real bug — OP2 registration targets from the WW dashboard are not persisting to DuckDB. Needs schema fix before next ingest.

[2026-04-08] Topic: MotherDuck dive creation capability investigation | Actions: Searched MotherDuck docs, found save_dive and get_dive_guide MCP tools exist on the MotherDuck remote MCP server (app.motherduck.com/mcp). Current config uses local mcp-server-motherduck (uvx) which only has SQL tools. Remote server needs to be added to MCP config for dive creation/editing. | Decisions: Need to add MotherDuck remote MCP server to .kiro/settings/mcp.json to enable programmatic dive creation. dive-v2.jsx is ready to deploy once the remote MCP connection is configured.

[2026-04-08] Topic: MotherDuck remote MCP server setup for dive creation | Actions: Searched for save_dive/get_dive_guide tools, confirmed they exist on MotherDuck remote MCP server (not the local mcp-server-motherduck). Found full tool list: save_dive, update_dive, list_dives, read_dive, get_dive_guide, share_dive_data. Added motherduck-remote MCP server config to .kiro/settings/mcp.json using mcp-remote proxy to https://mcp.motherduck.com/v1alpha/sse. | Decisions: Remote server needs OAuth browser auth on first connect. Once connected, can create/update dives programmatically. dive-v2.jsx ready to deploy via save_dive tool. Local duckdb MCP server kept for SQL queries.

[2026-04-08] Topic: MotherDuck remote MCP server added for dive creation | Actions: Searched for existing MotherDuck remote MCP config (not found — was on a previous recycled AgentSpace). Researched MotherDuck docs: remote server at mcp.motherduck.com has save_dive, update_dive, list_dives, read_dive, get_dive_guide tools. Added motherduck-remote MCP server to .kiro/settings/mcp.json using npx mcp-remote (same SSE bridge pattern as Hedy). | Decisions: Two MotherDuck MCP servers now configured: duckdb (local, SQL only) and motherduck-remote (remote, dive management + SQL). dive-v2.jsx ready to deploy via save_dive once the remote server authenticates.
