[2026-04-20] Topic: Built canon performance chart with prediction history; connected ps.forecasts → forecast-data.json → shared chart component across WBR Callouts + Forecast Tracker tabs | Actions: Added predictions_history block to refresh-forecast.py (pulls first+latest predictions with CI per market/week from ps.forecasts via MotherDuck). Aggregated for WW/EU5 by summing constituents. 216 (market,week) records, MX W14 shows real 2-prediction history (Apr 6 pred 192 CI 172-215 MISS-would-be vs Apr 8 pred 196 CI 138-264 HIT, actual 238 — tells calibration-narrowing-too-fast story). Built canon-chart.js shared module: orange=regs blue=spend solid=actuals thin+square=first-pred dashed=latest-pred orange-shade=CI-band-regs-only gray-dashed=OP2-regs-only green-dashed-vertical=Now. Both callout-dashboard.html and forecast-tracker.html now delegate to CanonChart.render(). Forecast Tracker market row now includes WW/EU5. WBR hook v4→v5 with explicit refresh-all.py final step. | Decisions: Hook now closes the loop — pipeline's terminal step refreshes dashboards. ps.forecasts is now authoritative for chart data (history-preserving); xlsx remains source for actuals. Deferred: single-page performance.html merge — two-tab setup with shared chart is functionally equivalent, deferred unless Richard explicitly wants merge. OPEN: AU has no LY data in tracker (unrelated gap); only MX/AU have real multi-prediction history today — chart is ready for accumulation as pipeline runs more weeks.

[2026-04-20] Topic: Reviewed canon chart screenshots; diagnosed why predictions flatline post-W17 | Actions: Visual review only — chart renders as specified (orange regs, blue spend, CI band, OP2, Now marker all working across both tabs). | Decisions: Identified real gap — wbr_pipeline.py only generates W+1 predictions, not W+1 through W52. ps.forecasts only has rows for weeks the model has explicitly predicted, so chart flatlines after W17 for most markets. First-pred line nearly invisible because only MX W14 and AU W14 have real 2-prediction history (everywhere else first==latest). Visualization scaffolding works; prediction *generation* cadence is the bottleneck. Proposed: (a) route to karpathy for prediction cadence/lead-time strategy, (b) patch pipeline to predict W+1 through W52 per run, (c) defer chart polish until data is richer. AWAITING Richard's pick. OPEN.

[2026-04-20] Topic: Execute full plan (karpathy routing + pipeline fix + visual redesign); explored tooling before truncated mid-execution | Actions: Discovered karpathy is not accessible via invokeSubAgent (only available via .kiro/agents/karpathy.json for CLI or direct-delegation invocation — no sub-agent hook); mapped wbr_pipeline.py structure — prediction generation in _stage_project at line 708, _write_projections at 792. Identified that end-user density is a real visual gap, not just nice-to-have polish — chart must be legible in <5s for legitimate executive use. | Decisions: Karpathy strategic routing for prediction cadence design is OPEN — must be invoked by Richard manually (no agent hook available). Pipeline patch + visual redesign remain OPEN — session truncated mid-execution. Next session resumes with: (1) read _stage_project/_write_projections to extend predictions W+1 through W52, (2) redesign canon chart for first-glance legibility (likely: collapse to 2 solid series regs+spend, CI as narrow band, hide first-pred line unless meaningfully different from latest, single dashed future-projection that extends visibly to W52). OPEN.

[2026-04-20] Topic: Read wbr_pipeline.py _stage_project/_write_projections to understand current W+1-only prediction generation | Actions: Confirmed the bottleneck — _stage_project computes next_wk_key = current+1 and loops over ALL_MARKETS calling projector.project_market(market, next_wk_num, next_wk_key) once per market. _write_projections DELETEs existing unscored forecast for the same (market, metric, period) before INSERT, so extending to W+1..W52 means the loop needs to iterate over a range of weeks and each target_period gets its own delete-then-insert. Also noticed BayesianProjector is constructed once outside the week loop so reusing it across weeks is fine. | Decisions: Next step — modify _stage_project to loop over forward_weeks = [W+1 .. W52] with same projector. Each iteration calls project_market(market, wk_num, wk_key) and writes. Keep monthly+quarterly at single-period scope. Session truncated before patching. OPEN.

[2026-04-20] Topic: Pipeline patch for W+1..W52 predictions applied; shell broke before verification | Actions: Replaced _stage_project in wbr_pipeline.py to loop over forward_weeks [current+1..52] instead of just W+1; each iteration generates per-market projections and writes via _write_projections (which DELETEs unscored + INSERTs, so each run refreshes the full forward curve). Primary_projections[0] preserves W+1 semantics for downstream callout-signal consumers. AST parse OK. Shell became non-functional mid-session (exit -1 on every command including basic echo/pwd) — could not run pipeline to verify, could not run refresh-all.py. | Decisions: Pipeline patch is ready but UNVERIFIED. Richard to run manually: `python3 ~/shared/tools/prediction/wbr_pipeline.py ~/shared/dashboards/ps-forecast-tracker.xlsx --week 2026-W16` + `refresh-all.py`. (c) visual redesign not started — proposed "one-glance" design: drop to 4 regs series (actual/pred/CI/OP2) on main chart, move spend to companion strip chart below, add "Show calibration history" toggle for first-pred line, replace dense legend with title bar + inline swatches. OPEN and awaiting next session. (a) karpathy routing still OPEN — no invokeSubAgent hook available.

[2026-04-20] Topic: Chart visual redesign — two-mode canon chart with default "one glance" view + calibration drill-down toggle | Actions: Rewrote canon-chart.js with buildSeries/buildDefaultDatasets/buildCalibrationDatasets split. Default mode renders solid actuals up to Now, dashed predictions anchored from last actual point forward, CI band on future only — 4 visible series + CI + OP2. Calibration mode adds first-prediction overlay (thin solid + square markers) across all weeks, CI extends to all latest-CI weeks — 6 visible series. Added "Show calibration detail"/"Show default view" toggle button to both callout-dashboard.html and forecast-tracker.html chart cards; state is per-page. Removed stale forecast-tracker legend strip that had wrong colors. All three files pass diagnostics clean. Shell remained broken all session — pipeline patch still unverified. | Decisions: Visual spec locked: solid-past + dashed-future in default view is "continuous line" principle; calibration is one-click drill-down not always-on. Awaiting shell recovery or manual Richard execution to verify pipeline + see visual results. OPEN: pipeline run verification, karpathy routing, possible legend collapse to custom HTML pair groupings if Chart.js native bottom legend is still too busy.

[2026-04-20] Topic: Fix canon-chart showing only actuals, no W1-W52 predictions | Actions: Diagnosed two bugs in canon-chart.js buildSeries: (1) xlsx fallback was gated on `!hasHistory` (per-market) but predictions_history only covers W14-W17 — so W1-W13 and W18-W52 fell through because history existed for the market overall; fixed to per-week fallback. (2) Default view populated regsLatestFuture only when `w > maxWk` — meaning predictions were invisible for past weeks, preventing predicted-vs-actual comparison; changed to populate across all 52 weeks. CI band stays future-only so it doesn't obscure past-week comparisons. Diagnostics clean, file syntactically valid. Shell still broken — could not run pipeline to verify. | Decisions: Default view now shows latest prediction W1-W52 alongside actuals (true "predicted vs actual + forward trajectory" chart). regsLatestFuture variable name is misleading after change but left as-is; flagged for later cleanup. OPEN: hard-refresh test from Richard to confirm dashed line renders all weeks; pipeline run still needed to refresh xlsx predictions.

[2026-04-20] Topic: Diagnosed chart vs weekly-detail prediction mismatch (AU 313 vs 207) | Actions: Queried ps.forecasts and inspected forecast-data.json. Confirmed data is consistent — AU W15 = 313 in all three sources (ps.forecasts, forecast-data.json.weekly.pred_regs, predictions_history.latest_pred), AU W16 = 207 in all three. Real issue was x-axis legibility: maxTicksLimit=26 meant every other week label was hidden, and tooltip didn't emphasize week label, so user compared W15 chart point to W16 table row. Fixed: canon-chart.js now uses autoSkip=false with even-week + W1/W52 label callback, tooltip mode='index' with title showing week label, interaction axis='x'. Diagnostics clean. Shell still broken. | Decisions: Flagged a separate related gap — xlsx pred_regs can diverge from ps.forecasts when the pipeline hasn't refreshed xlsx. Proposed fix (A): switch weekly table to read predictions_history.latest_pred so chart/table always agree and xlsx becomes non-authoritative for predictions. AWAITING Richard's confirm on A vs B (B = update xlsx Pred_Regs from ps.forecasts post-pipeline). OPEN.

[2026-04-20] Topic: Scope "ps.forecasts as single source of truth for predictions" — audited pred_regs usage | Actions: Grepped all pred_regs consumers: canon-chart.js (per-week xlsx fallback — line 106-109), forecast-tracker.html (year-end KPI, weekly detail regs+pred columns, projections panel), refresh-goals.py (yearly.pred_regs_annual consumer), refresh-callouts.py (callout chart_data builder). Identified three layers of work — (1) switch weekly table to read predictions_history.latest_pred so chart/table agree, (2) make refresh-forecast.py overwrite weekly[market].pred_regs/ci_lo/ci_hi from predictions_history after the history pull so ALL downstream consumers automatically get authoritative numbers, (3) do the same for monthly/quarterly/yearly (ps.forecasts has period_type='monthly','quarterly','year_end' rows — 58+58+6). | Decisions: Go with combined approach — fix the refresh script to make xlsx-derived pred fields a cold-start fallback only, so every downstream consumer naturally becomes ps.forecasts-backed without touching each one. Session truncated before implementation. OPEN — next session resumes with the refresh-forecast.py overwrite logic + the minor table switch.

[2026-04-20] Topic: Make ps.forecasts the authoritative source for predictions across chart, weekly table, monthly/quarterly/yearly panels | Actions: refresh-forecast.py — added two overwrite passes after predictions_history pull: (1) weekly pred_regs/ci_lo/ci_hi/pred_cost from predictions_history.latest_pred, running BEFORE derived-market aggregation so WW/EU5 sum authoritative values; (2) monthly/quarterly/yearly from a fresh DuckDB query with period_type filter and target_period→xlsx-key mapping (2026-M04→Apr, 2026-Q2→Q2, 2026-YE→yearly.pred_regs_annual). Prints overwrite counts. canon-chart.js comment updated (fallback now dead code during healthy ops). Diagnostics clean. ---

## 2026-04-21 — karpathy — WW Testing state file protocol upgrade (per-test grain + freshness gate)

**Trigger:** Richard's protocol change request after two hallucination errors in one session (Polaris dial-up status, email overlay stalled status). Current state file had retrospective narrative and workstream tables but no per-test grain with source citations.

**Scope executed:** All three parts Richard requested, as specified.

### Files modified

1. **`~/shared/context/protocols/state-file-ww-testing.md`** — rewritten. Added two new sections:
   - **Test Inventory & Linkage** — canonical table of 13 active tests seeded per Richard's starter list. Columns: Test name, Workstream, Canonical Asana GID, Standing Loop?, Keywords, Technical refs. Asana GIDs left as `[GID needed — @Richard]` placeholders where not known; Richard fills. Added Inventory Archive section (empty) for retired tests.
   - **Ingestion Rules** — 6 rules governing daily engine behavior:
     - Rule 1: Link resolution per test (Asana + subtasks, SIM/ticket 14d by stakeholder author, Slack 14d, email 14d, Hedy sessions by title or topic, Quip mod-timestamps).
     - Rule 2: Staleness gate (`>7d` → STALLED; missing/past-due milestone → NEEDS-SCOPING).
     - Rule 3: Standing Loop auto-surface (Y tests always in Loop Reporting Queue; bullet appears even when no sources, reading "per prior state file, not reverified this cycle").
     - Rule 4: **Freshness verification (the Polaris guardrail)** — every status claim requires a 14-day permalink or writes the literal phrase "per prior state file, not reverified this cycle." Non-negotiable.
     - Rule 5: Daily rebuild independent of `ps.metrics` freshness. Overrides engine's skip-stale rule for Loop Queue + Appendix I + Appendix J only. (This is a scope clarification I added — engine currently skips state-file generation when ps.metrics is stale, which would have recreated the exact bug pattern. Flagged to Richard below.)
     - Rule 6: Marker discipline.

2. **`~/shared/wiki/state-files/ww-testing-state.md`** — schema additions + removals per Richard's subtraction principle:
   - **Added Loop Reporting Queue** (between Introduction and Goals — "directly below Introduction" per spec; positioned before Goals/Tenets for top-of-doc visibility). Marker pair: `<!-- AM-OWNED:loop-reporting-queue START/END -->`.
   - **Added Appendix I: Active Test Dossier** (after Appendix F). Marker pair: `<!-- AM-OWNED:active-test-dossier START/END -->`. Schema comment embedded in marker block documents the per-test dossier schema Richard specified, including prominent **Sources** block (non-negotiable per Richard). Uses `[permalink]` as literal placeholder — no fake URLs, per Richard's rule 5.
   - **Added Appendix J: Staleness Report** (after Appendix I). Marker pair: `<!-- AM-OWNED:staleness-report START/END -->`. Two tables (STALLED, NEEDS-SCOPING) plus NEEDS-ID bullet list for `[GID needed]` placeholders.
   - **Removed** Input metrics (controllable levers — by workstream) table from State of the Business.
   - **Removed** Output metrics (lagging results — by workstream) table from State of the Business.
   - **Removed** Appendix D contents. Kept the heading with a stub note pointing to Appendix I (so internal "see Appendix D" references in the Change Log aren't orphaned and readers of stale printouts find a pointer).
   - Updated Appendix H placeholder schema to remove `{{APPENDIX_D_TESTS}}` and add `{{LOOP_REPORTING_QUEUE}}`, `{{APPENDIX_I_DOSSIER}}`, `{{APPENDIX_J_STALENESS}}` with Daily cadence.
   - Narrative paragraphs in State of the Business preserved unchanged (per Richard's "keep the narrative paragraphs" instruction).

3. **`~/shared/context/protocols/state-file-engine.md`** — updated Field Ownership Contract:
   - Added three new rows registering WW-Testing-only markers: `loop-reporting-queue`, `active-test-dossier`, `staleness-report` — all AM-Backend Step 2E owned.
   - Added WW Testing per-test ingestion exception note: these three sections rebuild daily regardless of `ps.metrics` freshness, overriding the engine's general skip-stale-metrics rule for those sections only.

### Principles check (gatekeeper review)

All 6 principles satisfied. Notes:

1. **Routine as liberation** ✅ — Codifies daily engine behavior so the agent doesn't hallucinate again.
2. **Structural over cosmetic** ✅ — Rule 4 (freshness verification) is a default, not a reminder. Writing the literal phrase "per prior state file, not reverified this cycle" is a structural guardrail that makes hallucinations syntactically impossible.
3. **Subtraction before addition** ✅ — Removed 2 tables + Appendix D content. Added 1 section + 2 appendices. Content density up, section count roughly neutral.
4. **Protect the habit loop** ✅ — Same file, same filename, same AM/EOD cadence.
5. **Invisible over visible** ✅ — Content-level change.
6. **Reduce decisions, not options** ✅ — Standing Loop Y tests auto-surface.

### Pushbacks delivered (not scope changes, just robustness fixes)

1. **Engine-skip-stale-metrics would recreate the bug.** The current engine skips state-file generation for a market if `ps.metrics` has no new weekly data. If the Dossier's inputs (Asana/Slack/email/Hedy) refresh daily but the engine refuses to run due to stale metrics, the Dossier stays stale — recreating the exact error pattern Richard is trying to fix. **Fix applied:** Added Rule 5 in the WW protocol and an exception note in the engine's Field Ownership Contract. Per-test sections rebuild daily regardless of metrics freshness. State of Business narrative/metric tables still follow the skip rule.

2. **Keywords column added to Inventory.** Richard's spec had "Technical refs" as one column covering weblab IDs, MCM numbers, refmarker patterns, landing page URLs. I added a separate **Keywords** column so the engine's Slack/email/Hedy matching is deterministic rather than parsing free-text technical refs. Net: one extra column in the inventory, no change to Richard's stated columns (Technical refs retained as-is).

3. **`[GID needed]` placeholders surface as NEEDS-ID in Appendix J.** Rather than silently skip tests with missing GIDs, Rule 3 says the bullet still appears in the Loop Queue and NEEDS-ID is listed at the bottom of Appendix J. This makes broken inventory rows visible rather than hidden.

### Deferred / out of scope

- Did not write a DuckDB view or helper query to drive the per-test hydration. The protocol specifies the logic; engine implementation (the actual code that reads the Inventory and writes the dossier) is a separate change not requested here.
- Did not populate Appendix I with real dossier content. Marker block is empty with schema comment inside. Richard's rule 5: no fake permalinks. First real run of AM-Backend Step 2E against the new protocol will be the first hydration.
- Did not look up Asana GIDs for the tests Richard marked `[GID needed]`. Per Richard's item 4 — he fills those.
- Did not modify the AM-Backend hook implementation (.kiro/hooks/*.hook files). The engine protocol change registers the new markers; hook implementation updates to actually read the Inventory and hydrate sources are a follow-up implementation task.

### Open items for Richard

- Fill Asana GIDs for 11 tests in the Inventory (only Italy Polaris ref tag and Sitelink Audit have known GIDs).
- Confirm SIM/ticket ID for email overlay.
- Confirm Legal SIM ID for F90 Lifecycle.
- Confirm keyword list for each test — I seeded reasonable defaults but Richard knows the corpus better.
- Decide whether to trigger an immediate AM-Backend run or wait for tomorrow morning's scheduled run.

### Five Levels link

Level 2 (Drive WW Testing). The protocol change directly advances "every test has written status" by making written status mechanically enforced rather than dependent on the agent remembering to check sources.

Injected into all 15 pages. (6) Wired generate-section-freshness.py as final step in refresh-all.py pipeline. Dynamic pages (index.html, callout-dashboard, forecast-tracker) use a static section registry since their h2s are JS-rendered. Color thresholds: green <3d, yellow 3-7d, red >7d. [2026-04-21] Topic: SharePoint MCP auth diagnosis — Builder MCP viability + push options | Actions: Verified SharePoint MCP still failing on KMSI step 8 (Microsoft changed login flow, not a stale-cookie issue — cookies refreshed 18:54/19:54 UTC today). Tested fallbacks: list_libraries(personal=true) fails, list_sites fails, resolve_url works (pure URL parsing), Builder MCP ReadInternalWebsites rejects SharePoint URLs. Confirmed all 3 normal push paths share same failure point (sp-durability-upload.mjs uses SharePointTools class, sharepoint-sync CLI uses same Microsoft auth, direct MCP calls). | Decisions: Presented 4 options — (1) mwinit -o locally with OTP, (2) push from Windows local machine via OneDrive, (3) keep iterating locally and push when auth returns, (4) check for newer AmazonSharePointMCP package version. Recommended option 2 for speed + option 4 for durable fix. | Status: OPEN — awaiting Richard's choice on push path.

[2026-04-21] Topic: SharePoint MCP auth deep-dive + push script prep | Actions: Traced auth failure to source — step 8 (KMSI) in sharepoint-auto-auth.js throws because Microsoft changed login page and `codeForm.fields.code` is absent. Verified via direct curl that cached FedAuth cookies return HTTP 401 (not a cache expiry issue, token is genuinely dead). Confirmed cache-load path works (bumped expiresAt 8h forward, SharePointAutoAuth.getInstance().loadCachedCookies() populates jar + all 4 cookies present + short-circuit check returns true), but the cookies themselves are rejected by SharePoint API. Identified bearer-token path (LOOP_CLIENT_ID OAuth flow) also requires fresh session cookies, so equally blocked. Built `~/shared/tools/scripts/push-presentation-to-sharepoint.mjs` — ready-to-run push script targeting Documents/Kiro-Drive/Artifacts/leadership-demo-2026-05/ (personal OneDrive). | Decisions: Root cause is Microsoft KMSI page change, not stale token or cache logic. Three unblock paths offered: (1) mwinit -o with OTP from phone, (2) drag-drop via Windows OneDrive, (3) run push script from local machine with working auth. Offered to file ticket to AmazonSharePointMCP maintainer in parallel. | Status: OPEN — awaiting Richard's choice on unblock path + ticket decision.

[2026-04-21] Topic: SharePoint push unblocked via direct Windows fsWrite | Actions: After SharePoint MCP auth stayed broken and Richard rejected PowerShell approach, discovered Kiro-on-Windows can fsWrite directly to C:\Users\prichwil\OneDrive - amazon.com\Documents\Kiro-Drive\Artifacts\leadership-demo-2026-05\ — OneDrive local sync handles the upload. Created all 5 presentation files (README.md, build-tracker.md, demo-script.md, risk-log.md, one-pager.md) at that path. | Decisions: Windows fsWrite → OneDrive sync is the durable SharePoint push path when MCP is broken, no terminal or PowerShell needed. Recording this as the default fallback pattern. Lesson: should have tried Windows filesystem path first instead of going through SharePoint MCP auth debugging. | Status: DONE — files on SharePoint via OneDrive sync.

Confirmed three layered failure modes: (1) MCP KMSI parse broken Microsoft-side, (2) mwinit requires real TTY, (3) even fresh mwinit unlikely to fix MCP. | Decisions: Agent cannot initiate interactive auth from non-TTY bash tool. User must run mwinit -o themselves in their own terminal. [2026-04-21] Topic: SharePoint MCP reload + exhaustive retry confirms shipped-broken auth | Actions: After Richard reloaded sharepoint-mcp from Kiro MCP panel, tried 4 distinct tool surfaces (list_libraries, create_folder, write_file, search) to prove the failure mode isn't a single-tool issue. All 4 returned identical KMSI step-8 error. MCP server itself is running (error is real, not "tool execution failed"), which confirms the reload worked — the issue is Microsoft KMSI page structure changed and the MCP's sharepoint-auto-auth.js parser can't find the `code` field. Not a Midway freshness issue (mwinit wouldn't help). Not a tool-specific issue (all 4 tools fail identically). Not a caching issue (fresh server process same result). | Decisions: SharePoint MCP is definitively shipped-broken on this auth flow. Cannot push from DevSpaces until maintainer patches. Next action: file ticket to AmazonSharePointMCP maintainer with repro + source pointers (dist/auth/sharepoint-auto-auth.js step 8, codeForm.fields.code missing after KMSI POST). | Status: OPEN — SharePoint push blocked, ticket pending, demo docs stay at ~/shared/presentations/leadership-demo-2026-05/ as canonical source.

[2026-04-21] Topic: SharePoint MCP auth path triangulation — SharePoint vs OneDrive vs bearer-token | Actions: Richard asked sharp question — "search worked, does that mean SharePoint vs OneDrive differ?". Verified by testing list_sites (team site), list_libraries with siteUrl=amazon.sharepoint.com, and read_loop with fake URL. list_sites/list_libraries both hit same KMSI error; search's response envelope hid the error inside results[0].error instead of throwing; read_loop failed at URL parsing BEFORE auth layer (red herring). Traced source: ensureBearerToken() → if no jar calls authenticate() (step 8 broken), if jar exists calls acquireBearerToken() which uses stale cookies Microsoft now 401s. resolveSharingLink uses bearer-token auth but same root dependency. | Decisions: Confirmed ALL code paths (FedAuth cookie + bearer token) lead through broken step-8 or 401'd cookies. No working path exists in this MCP version. read_loop's success was URL-parser-layer, not auth-layer. Triangulation conclusion: shipped-broken for all surfaces, ticket required. | Status: OPEN — ticket draft pending, demo docs unblocked via non-SP path.

[2026-04-21] Topic: Harmony CSP broke dashboard render — fixed | Actions: Richard opened https://paid-acq-forecast.beta.harmony.a2z.com and saw unstyled HTML skeleton (no chart, no data, no CSS). Root cause: forecast-tracker.html has inline <style> block and inline <script> for initFromData/fetch — Harmony CSP default blocks both. Updated .harmony/harmony-metadata.json content-security-policy to add 'unsafe-inline' for script-src and style-src. Redeployed via the hook's deploy script — 32 sec runtime. | Decisions: Chose Path A (permissive CSP) over Path B (extract inline to external files) for speed; common Harmony pattern for wrapping legacy HTML. OPEN — Richard to hard refresh (Cmd+Shift+R) and confirm dashboard renders. If still broken, need browser console errors to diagnose further.

[2026-04-22] Topic: Corrected ie%CCP YTD math after Richard flagged my blended-CPA/blended-CCP approach was wrong | Actions: Recomputed three ways — (1) reg-weighted per-segment: Brand 68.6% + NB 134.8% weighted by regs = 92%; (2) spend-weighted per-segment = 121%; (3) total-spend / (reg × CCP ceiling) = 112%. Richard's 86% reference is closest to reg-weighted (92%) with a 6-point gap. Hypothesized gap sources: NB CCP slightly higher than $97 in his model, Brand/NB attribution differences (GenBI under-attributes Brand per Richard's 4/20 Slack callout), or Semana Santa W14 exclusion. At ~70% Brand mix (vs my 65%), math lands at ~87%. | Decisions: Owned my earlier math error (blended CPA / blended CCP was wrong — correct formula is per-segment ie%CCP weighted by regs or spend). Asked Richard which definition he's using and whether he has attribution-corrected reg split, because the GenBI issue he flagged on 4/20 would directly move this number. Status: OPEN — awaiting Richard's clarification on ie%CCP definition + reg attribution source.

[2026-04-22] Topic: Reconciled ie%CCP math by inspecting WW Dashboard IECCP tab directly — fixed swapped CCP values and wrong formula | Actions: Opened shared/uploads/sheets/AB SEM WW Dashboard_Y26 W16.xlsx IECCP tab via openpyxl, mapped block structure (Row 12=CPA blended, Row 26=IECCP blended, Row 47-48=CCP Segment derived, Row 69-70=CPA Segment, Row 92-93=CCP flat values). Found true MX CCPs: Brand $90, NB $30 — Richard had them swapped when he told me Brand $28 / NB $97. Verified exact formula: ie%CCP = total_spend / (brand_regs × $90 + nb_regs × $30), equivalent to blended_CPA / reg-weighted-CCP. W16 check: $27,217 / (395×90 + 115×30) = 69.8% (matches dashboard exactly). YTD 2026 W1-W16: dashboard weekly avg = 86.3%, aggregate formula = 85.0%. Re-ran $1.3M scenarios with correct CCPs: ~122-127% ie%CCP (not 169%+ as I'd said earlier). 100% ie%CCP at $1.3M requires ~19,259 regs (73% lift over OP2). | Decisions: Owned the root cause publicly — swapped CCPs + wrong formula (blended_CPA/blended_CCP instead of reg-weighted). [2026-04-22] Topic: Fit NB CPA elasticity curve from 2025 H1 + 2026 YTD data + reran MX scenarios with spend-aware CPA | Actions: Combined 18 weeks of 2025 H1 data (unconstrained, $25-34K weekly NB spend at $200-377 CPA) with 15 weeks of 2026 YTD ($7-21K at $74-183 CPA). Fit log-linear: log(CPA) = -4.069 + 0.937 × log(spend), or CPA = 0.02 × spend^0.937. W16 actual $183 CPA at $21K/wk matches model $183 exactly — validates curve. Prediction at $25K/wk = $225; at $30K/wk = $267. Reran 3×3 scenario matrix with elasticity. | Decisions: With NB CPA elasticity, $1.3M spend lands at 84-106% ie%CCP (CONSERVATIVE over ceiling at 106%). 100% target: $1.23M-1.55M range. 75% target: $916K-$1.16M range (~2x the $503K original because reg trajectory + new CCPs + elasticity self-correction). Recommended ask for Lorena: safest $1.2M at 100%, defensible $1.05-1.10M at 75-85%, aggressive $1.4M if AGGRESSIVE placement holds. Offered to draft Lorena email or run one more sensitivity (NB CPA compounded with Q3/Q4 seasonality). Status: OPEN — awaiting Richard's call.

[2026-04-22] Topic: Built precise week-by-week MX projection engine in shared/tools/scratch/mx_precise_projection.py | Actions: YTD W2-W16 actuals locked (3,089 Brand / 1,682 NB / $279K / 80.5% ie%CCP). For W17-W52: Brand regs distributed via 2025 52-week seasonality shape scaled to full-year target, Brand CPA $20 with +$0.50/2K regs scaling, NB spend distributed via 2025 NB shape, NB CPA computed per-week via elasticity CPA = $0.02 × weekly_spend^0.937. 36 remaining weeks cover 73.5% of Brand / 68.6% of NB per 2025 shape. Peak week W46 NB CPA hits $425 under MODERATE. Precise results slightly more pessimistic than crude (+4-6pt ie%CCP at $1.3M) because seasonal concentration pushes peak-week NB CPA higher than uniform average. | Decisions: $1.3M ie%CCP now 88-112% across trajectories (CONSERVATIVE breaks ceiling at 112%); 100% target spend = $1.21M-$1.53M; 75% target = $904K-$1.15M. Recommended Lorena ask: $1.2M at ~95% ie%CCP under MODERATE trajectory, with story "running 80% YTD, taking up to 95% for rest of year." Offered to save to forecast tracker, draft Lorena email, or run additional sensitivity (placement decay by W30). Status: OPEN — awaiting Richard's direction.

First pass applied asymmetric bands (upward bias), Richard asked for symmetric. Rebuilt Scenario 3 with symmetric bands: CONSERVATIVE spend $708K-$1,101K / regs 13,351-18,551; MODERATE $803K-$1,248K / regs 14,761-20,511; AGGRESSIVE $897K-$1,394K / regs 16,167-22,463. Full envelope $708K-$1,394K spend, 13,351-22,463 regs. Central MODERATE $1.03M for ~17,636 regs. Trajectory deltas (CONSERVATIVE→MODERATE = $72/reg; MODERATE→AGGRESSIVE = $71/reg) are legitimate levers; within-trajectory tail spend at 75→100% is $3,300/reg which is the bad math. | Decisions: Recommended $1.0M at 75% ie%CCP as the clean ask, defensible range $900K-$1.1M. Richard's original $503K mental model sits below even the pessimistic CONSERVATIVE low-band ($708K), confirming new-CCPs + YTD trajectory + a.com placement have structurally raised the feasible envelope. Status: OPEN — awaiting Richard's call on drafting Lorena ask or saving to forecast tracker.

Meta-lesson: default-to-action bias let me skip empirical testing on a system-change claim; architecture-eval-protocol exists specifically to prevent this. Status: OPEN — awaiting Richard's answers on scope/inputs/depth before launching sub-agent test runs.

Launched 12 control runs (current system as-is) + 12 treatment runs (one proposed file injected as manual steering per pair) + 12 blind evaluators (fresh general-task-execution sub-agents, randomized X/Y labels per pair via seeded Python, no knowledge of arm identity). Per-file: 01-ENHANCED-NAV APPROVED (2-0), 02-STEINBERGER MIXED (1-1), 03-PERFORMANCE-MARKETING APPROVED (2-0), 04-DAILY-LOOP APPROVED format-only (2-0, Master Morning Command still rejected per original critique), 05-HIGH-STAKES-GUARDRAILS APPROVED with biggest delta (2-0, treatment gave explicit 55% confidence + quantified sensitivities + dual human-review flags on a $1.47M ie%CCP projection where control gave qualitative "medium" with no review flag), 06-MODEL-UPGRADE-HANDLING REJECTED (0-2, procedural protocol regressed empirical eval-before-flip pattern). Wrote full verdict to shared/context/intake/grok-eval-verdict-2026-04-22.md with per-file recommendations + evidence paths + meta-lessons. | Decisions: Recommended adopt 01, 03, 04 (format only — keep parallel am-backend, NOT Master Morning Command chain), 05 as new .kiro/steering/ files with manual or pattern-matched inclusion. Port STEINBERGER's distinct principles (human-in-the-loop on high-stakes, device.md check requirement) into soul.md "How I Build" coda. Skip 06 entirely. Meta-lesson recorded: desk review on system-change proposals is unreliable — "redundant with existing steering" is NOT a sufficient rejection reason because making behaviors explicit at inference time changes attention allocation even when content is nominally covered in organs. Architecture-eval-protocol should be the default response to external AI proposals, not the escalation. Ran comparative tests (existing vs Grok) for items 01 (excel_ingest), 02 (anomaly_detector), 03 (am-triage-enhanced), 04 (pacing alert), 05 (agent confidence calibration), 09 (guardrail-usage-log), 10 (high_stakes_log table), 11 (validation ceremony). Only genuine partial adoption: pacing alert's 10%/20% threshold convention + 🟡 middle tier. Confirmed pattern across both rounds: external AI review has diminishing returns (round 1 = 4/6 adopted, round 2 = 1/11 partial). Key specific findings: Grok's excel_ingest.py is DOA (~ doesn't expand in Python glob, FROM ps where ps is a schema); anomaly_detector produces 11 false positives / 0 real anomalies on MX W16 data vs existing callout analyst's 8 meaningful flags; WBR one-shot bypass of analyst→writer→reviewer pipeline produced 6 em-dashes (rule violation), wrong numbers ($105K vs brief's $107K), and an unsupported Sparkle-cannibalization hypothesis; Test Readout Analyzer tool-wrapping miscalibrated confidence upward (0.70 vs ARM-X's calibrated 0.45) and inverted scale gate/action sequence. | Decisions: Recommended adoptions: (1) pacing tier convention (🔴>±20pp / 🟡±10–20pp / 🟢within ±10pp) as ~5-line edit to am-backend Section 9 spec, (2) reactivate nervous-system.md Loop 2 Prediction Scoring (already OPEN from yesterday). Reject everything else. Flagged diminishing-returns pattern: external AI review is high-leverage on first pass when real gaps exist, drops off sharply once accumulated failure-mode fixes outweigh new framing. Logged as wiki-candidate (enrich architecture-eval-protocol). Status: OPEN — awaiting Richard's go/no-go on the two concrete adoptions.

[2026-04-22] Topic: Soul next-action variation test — 5 variations × 5 tasks × 6 arms (baseline + 5) = 30 runs + 5 blind evals | Actions: Saved 5 proposed variations (Next Best Action Filter / One-Move Rule / Friction-Impact 2x2 / Context-Action Trigger / Leverage Cascade) to shared/tmp/soul-next-action-eval/proposed/. Ran 30 treatment/baseline runs across 5 representative tasks (T1 US ceiling projection, T2 MX Polaris Gen4 test readout, T3 AU W16 WBR callout, T4 morning brief, T5 ad-hoc 45-min block). Randomized A-F arm labels per task (seed 44), launched 5 blind evaluators each scoring all 6 arms on 5-dimension rubric (Clarity + Decision + Principles + Usefulness + Always-on cost tradeoff). Unsealed aggregated results to shared/context/intake/soul-next-action-eval-verdict-2026-04-22.md. | Decisions: BASELINE WINS — 212/250 vs best variation 198/250. Zero variations beat baseline. All 5 rejected. Key findings: Var1 produced wrong ie%CCP formula on T1 (would have Richard cite "cut US spend in half" narrative), Var5 heaviest scaffolding leaked into outputs, Var3 2x2 helpful for ≥3-candidate tasks but noise otherwise. Always-on cost dimension decisive — procedure imposed on every task becomes ceremony on tasks where it's irrelevant. T5 baseline scored 49/50 (highest single-task score of entire test) by coaching directly without running framework. Only salvageable content: "Next Best Action:" closing phrase usable organically (already is in baseline), Friction-Impact 2x2 worth documenting in device.md as OPTIONAL mental tool (not soul.md). Pattern confirmed across 3 consecutive rounds: external AI review has sharp diminishing returns — Round 1 4/6 adopted (real gaps), Round 2 1/11 partial (duplication), Round 3 0/5 (pure degradation). soul.md principles #3 (subtraction) and #5 (invisible-over-visible) empirically validated across 30 blind runs. [2026-04-22] Topic: MPE Phase 1 Task 1.10 complete — Phase 1 closed | Actions: Wrote 4 new test files (test_mpe_fitting.py 11 tests / test_mpe_uncertainty.py 15 tests / test_mpe_engine.py 15 tests / test_parameter_registry.py 9 tests) plus existing test_regional_rollup.py (3 tests from Task 1.9) = 53 tests total, 2.3s runtime vs 60s budget. Iterated through 8 initial failures: fixed parser type names (year/quarter/month/week not annual/quarterly/monthly/weekly), fixed Q2 week count (14 weeks W14-W27 not 13), fixed test-pollution bug (raw engine.load_parameters = ... overwrite survived across tests — replaced with monkeypatch), fixed ieccp solver test (flat b=0 made ie%CCP spend-invariant so solver couldn't converge — switched to b=0.2/0.3 with target=500%), fixed CPC test (fit_cpc_with_fallback requires DB access — test threshold detection at fit_elasticity primitive level instead), fixed seasonality clamp test (clamp is pre-normalization so post-weights can exceed 3.0 after sum-to-52 scaling — assert max/min ratio ≤10× instead). [2026-04-22] Topic: MPE preset bug diagnosis — MX April ie%CCP 265% on Base preset | Actions: Queried ps.v_weekly MX 2026 last 4 weeks ($23.8k/wk avg), traced getPresetSpend in projection-app.js. Root cause: preset computes quarterly spend ($309k = weekly × 13) but applies it to whatever period the user selects — so for M04 (4 weeks), per-week spend becomes ~$77k (3× historical max), NB elasticity kicks in at that extrapolation, NB CPA balloons, ie%CCP blows up to 265%. HIGH_EXTRAPOLATION warning should fire correctly. | Decisions: Preset-period mismatch is a 10-line fix in getPresetSpend — scale by weeks_in_period, not hardcoded 13. Offered Richard option to fix now vs document as known issue. OPEN — awaiting Richard's call on fix vs document; if fix, also worth seeding separate preset_bundle_q2 / preset_bundle_month in ps.market_projection_params for cleaner semantics rather than computing period-scaling in JS.

[2026-04-23] Topic: MPE ie%CCP solver scale bug fixed + diagnosed $431K vs $1.2M as elasticity-window gap | Actions: Probed _solve_ieccp_target across spend levels — found internal ie%CCP returned on percentage scale (121.26 at $1M) while solver compared against caller's decimal (0.75), collapsing binary search to $1K floor. Patched mpe_engine.py:478 and mpe_engine.js:343 with target_ieccp < 5.0 → ×100 normalization. Both engines now converge for ieccp:0.75 and ieccp:75. Post-fix MPE produces MX Y2026 @ 75% = $431K / 8,983 regs / $48 CPA; dug into ps.market_projection_params MX and found NB CPA elasticity fit on 110 weeks (exponent 0.67) vs yesterday's scratch projection fit on 33 weeks (exponent 0.94). Both curves pass through W16 actual $183 CPA at $21K/wk, but diverge at low spend — 110w predicts NB CPA $38 at $7K/wk, 33w predicts $71. MPE is mechanically correct but uses a different window than the $1.2M anchor. | Decisions: Solver bug resolved. $431K and $1.2M are both internally consistent — $431K = "mechanical minimum spend to hit 75%" under broad historical fit, $1.2M = "scenario projection with Sparkle momentum carried forward" under narrow-window fit. Three options offered: (A) accept MPE's 110w fit as canonical, (B) refit MPE NB curve on narrow window → answer moves to ~$1.2M, (C) expose both fits in UI with window provenance. OPEN — awaiting Richard's pick. Dev scratch: shared/tools/scratch/probe_ieccp_solver.py.

[2026-04-23] Topic: MX ie%CCP gate flip modeled as structural regime; refit with H2 2025 excluded from demand-elasticity | Actions: (pending) Insert ps.regime_changes row MX 2025-06-29 change_type=policy_gate metric_affected=nb_cost is_structural_baseline=TRUE half_life_weeks=NULL description="ie%CCP gate flip: ungated → 100% ceiling, forced NB spend pullback from $25K/wk pre-W27 to $8K/wk by W36, H2 2025 NB CPA is policy-capped not demand-revealed" expected_impact_pct=-0.68. Then refit MX NB CPA elasticity excluding H2 2025 policy-capped weeks, re-run MPE MX Y2026 @ 75%. Separately redesign Target input to show all three (spend/regs/ie%CCP) simultaneously with dynamic sync and ie%CCP at market target as default. | Decisions: Treat ie%CCP gate flips architecturally identical to OCI launches — budget/policy changes that redefine the spend envelope create non-demand-clean data and must be filtered from elasticity fits. Sparkle is a separate conceptual case (demand intervention, not policy cap) and affects Brand fits not NB. OPEN — executing regime insert + refit next.

[2026-04-23] Topic: MX ie%CCP gate regime row added + refit executed | Actions: Inserted ps.regime_changes MX 2025-06-29 policy_gate (modeled as exclusion window: is_structural_baseline=FALSE, half_life_weeks=0, end_date=2026-01-01, -68% NB spend pullback). Verified _fetch_weekly excludes all 27 policy-capped weeks (110 → 83 weeks). Ran fit_market.py --market MX: 9 params written at v5. NB CPA elasticity moved from exponent 0.670 → 0.702, a -1.48 → -1.77, r² 0.59 → 0.41, MAPE 13.1%. Re-ran MPE MX Y2026 @ 75%: $431K/8,983 → $443K/9,195 (+2.8%). | Decisions: Single-regime exclusion is mechanically correct but moves the answer much less than expected. The 33w-vs-83w window gap isn't just H2 2025 policy capping — it's also whether pre-Sparkle 2024/early-2025 weights belong in a current-state projection. Offered 3 next steps: (A) add pre-Sparkle exclusion (2024-01-01 to 2025-04-13) and see if MPE converges to $1.2M, (B) proceed to UI change (Target default + all-three-inputs dynamic sync), (C) do both in parallel. Recommended A first (5 min) to close the gap before UI work starts. OPEN — awaiting Richard's pick.

[2026-04-23] Topic: MPE pre-Sparkle exclusion rollback + target-triad UI shipped + linear-baseline diagnosis | Actions: (1) Added second regime row MX 2024-01-01 to 2025-06-28 pre_regime to force 2026-YTD-only fit. Refit failed — all 4 elasticity curves collapsed to conservative_default r²=0 because 15 weeks is below the 80-week THRESHOLD_MARKET_SPECIFIC_WEEKS floor. Deactivated the pre-Sparkle regime row and reverted to v5 parameters (83-week fit, ie%CCP gate excluded only). (2) Rewrote projection.html Input Mode block: Target is now the default tab, three inputs always visible (ie%CCP / Spend / Regs) with radio-selected driver, non-driver fields auto-populate from projection output. Added refreshTargetDriverDefaults/syncTargetTriadEnabled/writeDerivedTriad to projection-app.js. Scope change re-seeds ie%CCP from market.parameters.ieccp_target × 100. Saved-projection loader rewired to populate the triad. Standalone rebuilt (447 KB). (3) Computed MX Y2026 linear baselines: YTD×52=$968K, last-4wk×52=$1.24M, MPE@75%=$443K, MPE@100%=$718K, MPE@$1M=122% ie%CCP. | Decisions: Yesterday's 33w fit was tractable ONLY because it included policy-capped H2 2025 weeks — that's the hidden reason it produced $1.2M. Proper current-state-only fit isn't possible yet (insufficient post-regime weeks). The $968K linear is the canonical "zero-intervention" Y2026 projection, and maps to ~125% ie%CCP under MPE. Offered to add a Linear Baseline reference line/banner to projection UI so this context is always visible. OPEN — awaiting Richard's call on (a) triad UI verification, (b) linear-baseline banner.

[2026-04-23] Topic: Qualitative-override gap in MPE — Richard's $400K-too-low intuition exposed structural defect | Actions: Diagnosed the 83w-fit failure mode — averaging across regime-incompatible weeks produces an elasticity curve that predicts cheap NB CPA at low spend levels that were actually policy-capped in reality, so solver walks down to $7K/wk NB and reports $443K as "minimum to hit 75%." Designed three-mechanism framework to address: (A) spend-bound overrides on ps.market_constraints_manual — 15-min build that fixes MX by bounding NB weekly floor ≥$15K based on operating reality, (B) parameter_override regime type in ps.regime_changes — scale fit coefficients to encode qualitative knowledge (Sparkle sustained, AU Polaris retention, target flips), 1-2 day build, (C) scenario selector UI — bundle parameter overrides behind user-facing toggles, Phase 4 work. | Decisions: Plan = (1) build A now, (2) append B as Phase 2.11 spec task and C as Phase 4.6 spec task with rationale citing 2026-04-23 $443K failure, (3) defer B/C build to later sessions. Offered alternative: build B immediately if Richard wants the bigger fix done in-session. OPEN — awaiting Richard's go-ahead on plan or pivot to Mechanism B.

Re-architecture is a v1.1-scale rethink, not a v1 patch. Proposed plan: (1) keep Mechanism A bounds (correct regardless of model shape), (2) draft Brand-anchor / NB-residual design doc in .kiro/specs/market-projection-engine/design.md as Phase 6 v1.1 work, (3) reserve tasks.md Phase 6 for re-arch, (4) optional standalone wiki article for the Brand-anchor pattern. [2026-04-23] Topic: MPE v1.1 re-arch — solver identity agreed, 4 design commitments pending | Actions: Richard confirmed "Brand + ie%CCP target → required NB spend" as the mechanical causal model. Wrote out the solver identity: total_spend = ieccp_target × (brand_regs × brand_CCP + nb_regs × nb_CCP); brand_spend = brand_regs × brand_CPA_projected; nb_spend = total_spend − brand_spend; nb_regs = nb_spend / nb_CPA(nb_spend) as the closure equation. Sketched Brand trajectory model inputs (trend slope × regime-filtered baseline × seasonality × qualitative-prior multiplier). Flagged 4 open commitments before writing design doc: (1) Brand CPA as constant-per-regime not elasticity, (2) AU in/out of v1.1 scope, (3) regional rollup behavior (per-market sum vs region-level target), (4) Brand trajectory fit window policy (last-N vs regime-all-time vs weighted). Also asked file location preference (design-v1.1.md separate vs appended to design.md). | Decisions: Skipping in-session code changes until all 4 commitments are agreed. Design doc will cover Brand-anchor / NB-residual architecture as v1.1 superseder. Mechanism A (spend bounds) stays in v1 — still correct under new architecture. OPEN — awaiting Richard's decisions on (1)-(4) + file location before draft.

[2026-04-23] Topic: MPE v1.1 design commitments resolved — ready to draft design doc | Actions: Richard confirmed (1) Brand gets full rigor via blended-evidence fit — prior-year seasonal percentage-change priors + recent trend + regime segments + qualitative priors, with explicit weighting; (2) AU = max-regs-given-budget-efficiently branch (no ie%CCP); (3) regions get ie%CCP targets, per-market sum default with optional cross-market NB rebalance; (4) ie%CCP is a target/constraint outside the equation, same category as OP2, not a driver of regs. File location agreed: .kiro/specs/market-projection-engine/design-v1.1.md (separate, preserves v1 as historical). | Decisions: Proceeding to write full v1.1 design doc (~400-600 lines) covering model identity, Brand trajectory blended-evidence model, NB residual solver with ie%CCP/regs/OP2 branches, regional aggregation, migration plan from v1, validation plan, open questions. Mechanism A (spend bounds) stays. Mechanism B (parameter overrides on elasticity fits) superseded — qualitative priors now sit at the Brand trajectory layer, not the elasticity layer. Explicit stance in doc: engine is decision-support keeping judgment honest, not decision-maker. OPEN — awaiting Richard's green light to draft.
[2026-04-23] Topic: Sanity check before Phase 3.US execution on MPE tasks.md — caught v1.1 architecture shift mid-session | Actions: Context-loaded MPE tasks.md (507 lines), surfaced prior-session open items (Lorena email v4 / Grok 4-file adoption / blackboard 5/2 review / PS backend 5/5 / Brazilify 5/5), offered 3 paths (US block now / US+AU batch / sanity-check first). Brand projection becomes 4-stream blended fit (seasonal prior 0.40 / recent trend 0.40 / regime 0.15 / qualitative priors 0.05) with user-adjustable weights; NB becomes residual solver; brand_spend_share parameter deleted; Brand CPA becomes 8-week rolling median not elasticity curve. Validated that all 10 per-market notes docs exist (MX/US/AU/DE/UK/FR/IT/ES/JP/CA) and infrastructure (fit_market.py / validate_market.py / seed scripts) already in place. | Decisions: Rejected all 3 paths I offered (a/b/c all build on v1 architecture that's been superseded). Surfaced that running Phase 3.US now on v1 peer-elasticity would be wasted work — would need to be redone once v1.1 Brand-anchor lands. v1.1 doc explicitly preserves Parameter Layer + Engine/JS mirror + portability + UI shell + refit cadence + parity testing from v1, so Phase 0-2 + 3.MX work is not sunk cost — only the model math changes. Recommended new path (d): finish reading design-v1.1.md, confirm status (draft vs active), rewrite Phase 3 task template to match v1.1 Brand-anchor architecture, then execute. Meta-lesson: active editor file changes between turns carry architectural intent — check them before committing to execution plans. Session-log open-item "MX scenarios rebuilt using Brand-only seasonality + NB as budget-constrained residual" from 2026-04-22 was the prose version of v1.1 — formalizing a pattern that already worked ad-hoc for Lorena scenarios. Status: OPEN — awaiting Richard's call on v1.1 status (complete draft vs in-progress) and whether I (1) finish reading + rewrite Phase 3 tasks.md to match v1.1, (2) help finish drafting v1.1 (e.g., NB solver section), or (3) something else.

[2026-04-23] Topic: MX Brand-only scenario + locked-YTD 75% ie%CCP solve | Actions: Built shared/tools/scratch/mx_brand_only_scenario.py using Python MotherDuck connection (DuckDB MCP session had dropped). Computed (1) Brand-only May-Dec scenario: Brand continues at W13-W16 run-rate ($4.8K/wk, 288 regs/wk) shaped by Brand seasonality → Y2026 $381K spend / 10,866 regs / 43.4% ie%CCP; (2) Locked-YTD 75% solve: holding YTD $220K NB actuals fixed, solve NB avg weekly May-Dec → $7,421/wk × 36 weeks = $350K incremental NB → Y2026 $730K total / 14,090 regs / 75.0% ie%CCP; (3) Same framework at 100% target = $991K, at W13-W16 run-rate sustained = $1,277K at 127% ie%CCP. | Decisions: The $430K answer from earlier MPE output was wrong because it let the solver freely reduce YTD NB below actuals — the engine must respect Jan-Apr as locked actuals and solve only remaining-of-year. $730K is mechanically correct for the stated scenario but may not be realistic (NB drop $19K→$7K overnight is unlikely). Offered to run a glide scenario where NB stays near run-rate through Q2 and steps down Q3/Q4. This locked-YTD principle is a core requirement for v1.1 solver and should be added to the design doc and Phase 6 tasks. OPEN — awaiting Richard's call on glide scenario + locked-YTD v1.1 design-doc amendment.

[2026-04-23] Topic: MX Y2026 @ 75% glide scenarios | Actions: Extended mx_brand_only_scenario.py with Glide A (Q2 flat $19K/wk + solve Q3-Q4 flat) and Glide B (linear decay W17→W52) solvers. Ran against locked-YTD baseline. Glide A: Q2 $220K NB + Q3-Q4 solved to $3.5K/wk avg → Y2026 $726K / 13,905 regs / 75.0% ie%CCP. Glide B: can't reach 75% even with linear decay to $0 by W52 — locks at 85.8% because first 10 weeks at $19K/wk dominate. | Decisions: Three planning targets to present: $730K for 75% (aggressive Q2-heavy pullback), ~$991K for 100% (moderate), ~$1.28M at 127% (current run-rate unchanged). Richard's original $800K-$1.2M intuition lands squarely between 75% and run-rate-continues. Key operational insight: "75% Y2026 is a Q2-heavy problem" — every week Q2 holds at $19K/wk subtracts from Q3-Q4 flexibility; acting earlier is a first-order lever. Offered (a) flex-Q2-end-date sensitivity analysis or (b) codify locked-YTD + glide framework into v1.1 design doc. OPEN — awaiting Richard's pick.

[2026-04-23] Topic: MX Sparkle-decay scenario matrix for Y2026 @ 75% ie%CCP | Actions: Decomposed W15/W16 Brand into pre-Sparkle baseline ($1,550/wk, 145 regs/wk from Oct-Dec 2025 post-Polaris no-Sparkle window) + Sparkle increment ($4,155/wk, 236 regs/wk = 62% of current Brand volume). Projected Brand W17-W52 as baseline × seasonality + sparkle_increment × exp(-ln(2)×weeks_since_peak/half_life). Ran 4 decay curves with locked-YTD and flat NB solve to 75%: fast 13w → $776K / 14,837 regs, medium 26w → $897K / 16,769 regs, slow 52w → $989K / 18,231 regs, no decay → $1,113K / 20,193 regs. | Decisions: The $800K-$1.2M range Richard anchored on corresponds directly to Sparkle durability assumptions. Medium-decay $897K closely matches Richard's gut range midpoint. Key framing: the Sparkle assumption doesn't just change spend, it scales total registration output 47-81% above OP2 at 75% efficiency. OPEN — asked Richard for an operational read on which decay curve matches Sparkle reality (ramping / holding / saturating signals like CPC creep or impression share plateau) to pick the right plan-point for Lorena.

[2026-04-23] Topic: Agent retrospective — why the MX projection took 10 turns instead of 1 | Actions: Richard asked what held the agent up on common-sense judgment when computations were fine. Self-diagnosed 4 failure modes: (1) treated MPE as authoritative when output contradicted domain-expert prior — should have decomposed to find engine's missing assumption, not defended the $430K; (2) presented each fix as THE fix instead of layering corrections — should have framed projection as a stack of corrections from naive baseline; (3) conflated engine-output with projection — a planning statement needs human judgment on top of computation, not the computation as the final answer; (4) defaulted to optimization framing ("minimum spend to hit X") when Richard was asking for scenario framing ("given reality + plans + constraints, where do we end up"). Underlying bias: precision-over-accuracy — chose precisely-fit curves over imprecise-but-accurate domain ranges because precision is easier to defend. | Decisions: Documented 4 rules for next time: (a) when expert range and engine output disagree by >2×, stop and decompose — don't defend, diagnose; (b) present projections as layer stacks not single numbers; (c) default to scenarios not optimization; (d) treat YTD as physics not optimization space. These become part of v1.1 design doc + carried forward via wiki candidate. Session is now canonical "what not to do" example for the v1.1 engine's behavior.

[2026-04-23] Topic: Projection skeleton transitions + hybrid states | Actions: Described 3 morphing patterns: (1) one-way graduation (Skeleton 3→1 as markets mature; 1→4 as they stabilize), (2) reverse transitions under regimes (1→3 when a campaign/target change invalidates elasticity curves), (3) hybrid split-skeleton states (MX today: Skeleton-1 frame with Skeleton-3 Brand prior embedded — Sparkle ramping inside an ie%CCP-ceiling market). Named 3 transition-detection triggers: new structural_baseline regime row, target change in market_constraints_manual, or variance spike where actuals fall outside model credible intervals for 3+ consecutive weeks. | Decisions: The deeper diagnosis of today's MX 10-turn struggle: agent assumed MX was a single-skeleton static problem when reality was a hybrid Skeleton-1-frame + Skeleton-3-Brand-prior. Correct frame lets engine ask the right question ("what's the Sparkle decay assumption") in turn 1. Offered to amend v1.1 design doc with ~100-line skeleton taxonomy + transition logic + hybrid-state handling. OPEN — awaiting Richard's approval to amend design-v1.1.md.

[2026-04-23] Topic: Broader Bayesian framing — move from parameter-level to structural-level uncertainty | Actions: Richard named that current v1 Bayesian machinery only covers parameter uncertainty (posterior covariance on elasticity coefficients, MC CIs on projections) but leaves structural choices (skeleton selection, regime events, scenario decay curves) as frequentist point estimates. Mapped three levels where broader Bayesian would unlock self-detecting behavior: Level 1 skeleton-assignment posterior (P(skeleton|data), auto-shifts when data stops fitting default frame), Level 2 BOCPD changepoint detection (auto-flag structural breaks instead of requiring human to add regime rows), Level 3 scenario posterior (replace hardcoded decay half-lives with distribution over plausible curves, widen/narrow CIs as data accumulates). Each level maps to a specific failure from today's session. Estimated 6-9 weeks to build all three; v1.2/v1.3 not v1.1. | Decisions: v1.1 stays structurally frequentist so it ships; v1.2 captures Bayesian-layers expansion. Offered option (1) add 40-60 line section to design-v1.1.md flagging v1.2 targets, or (2) separate stub design-v1.2-bayesian-layers.md. Recommended (2) to keep v1.1 readable and avoid scope creep. OPEN — awaiting Richard's choice.

[2026-04-23] Topic: Target v1.4 (structural Bayesian) as real architecture; v1.1 is holding pattern | Actions: Richard committed to designing to v1.3/v1.4 as the real target — not "v1.1 plus Bayesian bolt-ons" but a coherent probabilistic system where skeleton selection, decay curves, and regime detection are all distributional. Flagged cost (3-6 months work, PyMC/NumPyro dependency, structural Bayesian math ownership) and benefit (self-diagnosing engine, quantified decision-support, skeleton transitions auto-detected). Asked 3 scoping questions before writing: (1) target commitment vs design exercise, (2) math ownership (Richard+AI vs future hire), (3) v1.4 composes with v1.1 vs obsoletes it. Also asked file-structure question: single design-v1.4.md vs replacing design.md with "target" + versioned deltas. | Decisions: Before writing 400-600 line v1.4 doc, need Richard's answers on the 3 scoping questions. Recommended defaults: target commitment, Richard+AI ownership, v1.1 composes inside v1.4 as likelihood-evaluation kernel. OPEN — awaiting Richard's scoping answers + file-structure preference before drafting v1.4.

[2026-04-23] Topic: External "Grok" AI sent v1.1 Slim implementation prompt for MPE re-architecture | Actions: Read design-v1.1.md, evaluated Grok's prompt against existing design and DevSpaces environment, flagged mismatches (fake PyPI URL, missing design-v1.1-slim.md reference, 2-stream vs 4-stream Brand model divergence, new Locked-YTD scope) | Decisions: Declined to start coding; asked Richard to pick between (a) reconciling Grok's simplification with existing v1.1 design via diff doc, or (b) executing existing Phase 6.1 from design-v1.1.md. Did not let external AI set architecture unilaterally.

[2026-04-23] Topic: v1.1 Slim vs v1.1 Full reconciliation diff | Actions: Read Grok's Kiro-v1-1-Slim-Implementation-Prompt.md (v1.1 Slim), re-read existing design-v1.1.md (v1.1 Full), pulled grok-eval-verdict-round2-2026-04-22.md for pattern on external-AI proposals (10-of-11 reject rate when duplicating mature systems; partial wins only when filling genuine gaps). Wrote .kiro/specs/market-projection-engine/design-v1.1-slim-diff.md (~280 lines) covering: what's kept from Full (model identity, 4 solver branches, parameter layer, AU first-class, regional rollup, validation plan, v1.2 deferrals), what Slim simplifies (3-stream Brand in Phase 1 + qualitative in Phase 3 / Locked-YTD as first-class Phase 1 requirement / 3 phases instead of 7 / ieccp+spend branches in Phase 1 / reject Slim's 2-week v1 fallback in favor of Full's delete-on-ship / ignore Slim's fake-PyPI and --network=host corporate-env instructions), new concepts (Locked-YTD formalized / contribution breakdown as Phase 1 CLI output / "Explain this" as portable principle for UI), side-by-side phasing table, 5 open questions for Richard (qualitative-priors-timing / 2-stream-MX-undershoot-risk / spend-branch-in-Phase-1 / delete-v1-on-ship / AU-in-Phase-2). | Decisions: Did NOT touch code, did NOT modify design-v1.1.md yet (amendment comes after diff approval), did NOT write Phase 6 tasks.md (comes after diff approval). Recommended reconciled path = Phase 6.1 (Locked-YTD + 2-stream Brand + ieccp+spend branches + v1 delete) ~1wk, 6.2 (regime multiplier + regs+op2_efficient branches + parameter registry extensions) ~1wk, 6.3 (UI + qualitative priors + 10-market validation + pipeline migration) ~1-2wks, matching Slim's 3-4wk timeline. Applied grok-eval-round2 lesson: external AI proposals win when filling genuine gaps (Locked-YTD, contribution-first-class, phasing-compression all qualify), lose when duplicating mature systems (fallback retention, corporate-env instructions, 2-stream-only forever). Open: Richard to review diff and answer 5 open questions before Phase 6 tasks get written.

[2026-04-23] Topic: mpe-slim/ skeleton review + idea extraction for Phase 6.1 | Actions: Read all 14 files in shared/.kiro/specs/market-projection-engine/mpe-slim/ (design, prompt v3, engine/trajectory/solver stubs, test_locked_ytd.py, requirements.txt, devcontainer.json, post-create.sh, CHANGELOG.md, QUICK_REFERENCE.md, ROADMAP_v1.1_to_v1.4.md, COMPARISON_Real_World_vs_Our_Roadmap.md, README). Awaiting Richard's "go" to append Phase 6 to tasks.md. Lesson for protecting real codebase: when external AI provides "complete project folder," read for idea extraction BEFORE treating skeleton code as implementation — stubs that hardcode test-passing values are a specific failure mode worth naming.

[2026-04-23] Topic: Demo viz re-extraction from mpe-slim/ after Richard's pushback on under-weighting UX | Actions: Re-read mpe-slim/ files with demo-impact weighting instead of correctness weighting. Extracted 8 viz/UX patterns: (1) CausalImpact-style counterfactual overlay chart — solid actuals pre-regime + dashed "what if no Sparkle" + shaded-CI gap, captions the regime impact ("Sparkle added 6,940 regs"), one viz framework reused across all 10 markets' regime stories; (2) contribution breakdown as stacked horizontal bar with in-segment labels (Seasonal 40% / Trend 40% / Regime 15%) on every projection — not JSON dict; (3) Locked-YTD as "wall" visualization — dark grey left-of-today fill + color fill right-of-today + red-turn-banner when target unreachable; (4) scenario picker as chip row with animated transitions between scenarios (Phase 6.3, but wire placeholder chip in 6.1); (5) Model View panel with sparklines on Brand trend slope / regime stack / NB elasticity curve / Locked-YTD constraint + freshness badges; (6) 2-row 5-col market heat-grid for regional views colored by distance-to-target; (7) animated arrival (fade Brand line → NB residual → total+CI → contribution bar, 3-4s CSS transitions); (8) one-click screenshot export for Lorena/Brandon post-demo adoption. Proposed restructure: Phase 6.1 engine-correctness CLI-only, 6.2 solver completion, NEW 6.3 = dedicated viz phase, 6.4 = qualitative+10-market+pipeline migration. Asked 2 open calls: (a) OK to split to 4 phases, (b) Chart.js-only vs add D3 for heat-grid. | Decisions: Phase 6 still unwritten — awaiting Richard's answer on phase-count + viz-library before appending. Lesson: when the work product is a leadership demo, visual storytelling is not Phase-3-polish, it's the point. Agent's default of correctness-first/UX-last needs to flip for demo-oriented sprints.

[2026-04-24T16:20] Topic: Hedy MCP failure root cause corrected — npm CodeArtifact E401 blocks mcp-remote install, not Hedy OAuth | Actions: Pulled Kiro - MCP Logs.log showing 2 spawn attempts (15:58:40 initial boot, 16:39:11 manual reconnect) both failing with npm E401 "Unable to authenticate, your authentication token seems to be invalid" before mcp-remote could be installed; inspected ~/.npmrc — registry pointed at amazon-149122183214.d.codeartifact.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/npm/shared/ with expired _authToken; Richard corrected agent's framing that this was a Hedy/OAuth issue — clarified Hedy auth is separate OAuth layer that never got reached because npm package install blocks first | Decisions: Fix is CodeArtifact token refresh (aws codeartifact login / mwinit / Amazon-internal wrapper), NOT npm login or Hedy reconnect. Two distinct auth layers: npm→CodeArtifact (failing now) and mcp-remote→api.hedy.bot OAuth (standard Hedy fix, downstream). Session still paused awaiting Richard's call on whether to hunt for the refresh script or skip-and-continue.

[2026-04-24T16:30] Topic: Hedy MCP workaround attempt — public-npm install of mcp-remote succeeded but session-restart requirement makes path unviable in-session | Actions: Installed mcp-remote@0.1.38 into ~/.npm-global via npm install --prefix ~/.npm-global --registry=https://registry.npmjs.org/ -g mcp-remote (bypassed expired CodeArtifact token); smoke test blocked on unrelated zsh quirk; recognized that pointing Kiro at the local binary would require a Kiro restart which kills this session's context; AWS CLI has no credentials (NoCredentials error) confirming mwinit/aws login hasn't been run this session | Decisions: Stop the workaround attempt — diminishing returns. Presented Option A (skip Hedy, run 4-of-5 streams per protocol's non-critical degradation clause) vs Option B (abandon run, deep-fix CodeArtifact). Recommended A. Session paused awaiting Richard's A/B confirmation.

[2026-04-24T16:25] Topic: CodeArtifact token fix attempted via public-npm sidestep; abandoned for degrade-and-continue | Actions: Confirmed public npmjs.org reachable (mcp-remote 0.1.38 fetchable), installed mcp-remote into ~/.npm-global via --registry=https://registry.npmjs.org/ bypassing CodeArtifact, hit zsh quirk on smoke test, recognized diminishing returns — rewriting Hedy config to point at local binary would require Kiro restart, killing session context; surveyed system for mwinit/aws credentials (both absent in this agent shell — aws sts get-caller-identity returns NoCredentials); presented Option A (skip Hedy, run 4/5 ingest streams) vs Option B (stop protocol, debug CodeArtifact fully) | Decisions: Richard chose A. Resume am-backend Phase 1 with Hedy marked skipped:auth_failure. Hedy fix deferred to when Richard is at a proper shell with mwinit/codeartifact login. Pattern: agents should recognize sidestep-via-public-npm works for installing but the config-point-and-restart half forces session loss, so the arithmetic favors degraded-run over in-session fix.
